fields.description: Optional. The name of this field. Can be absent for struct fields.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The data type of a variable such as a function argument. Examples include: * INT64: `{"typeKind": "INT64"}` * ARRAY: { "typeKind": "ARRAY", "arrayElementType": {"typeKind": "STRING"} } * STRUCT>: { "typeKind": "STRUCT", "structType": { "fields": [ { "name": "x", "type": {"typeKind": "STRING"} }, { "name": "y", "type": { "typeKind": "ARRAY", "arrayElementType": {"typeKind": "DATE"} } } ] } } * RANGE: { "typeKind": "RANGE", "rangeElementType": {"typeKind": "DATE"} }

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_configuration_query_systemVariables_types_additionalProperties_typeKind
types.description: Required. The top level type of this field. Can be any GoogleSQL data type (e.g., "INT64", "DATE", "ARRAY").

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_configuration_query_tableDefinitions
types.description: Optional. You can specify external table definitions, which operate as ephemeral tables that can be queried. These definitions are configured using a JSON map, where the string key represents the table identifier, and the value is the corresponding external data configuration object.

fields.name: autodetect
fields.description: Try to detect schema and format options automatically. Any option specified explicitly will be honored.

fields.name: compression
fields.description: Optional. The compression type of the data source. Possible values include GZIP and NONE. The default value is NONE. This setting is ignored for Google Cloud Bigtable, Google Cloud Datastore backups, Avro, ORC and Parquet formats. An empty string is an invalid value.

fields.name: connectionId
fields.description: Optional. The connection specifying the credentials to be used to read external storage, such as Azure Blob, Cloud Storage, or S3. The connection_id can have the form `{project_id}.{location_id};{connection_id}` or `projects/{project_id}/locations/{location_id}/connections/{connection_id}`.

fields.name: dateFormat
fields.description: Optional. Format used to parse DATE values. Supports C-style and SQL-style values.

fields.name: datetimeFormat
fields.description: Optional. Format used to parse DATETIME values. Supports C-style and SQL-style values.

fields.name: decimalTargetTypes
fields.description: Defines the list of possible SQL data types to which the source decimal values are converted. This list and the precision and the scale parameters of the decimal field determine the target type. In the order of NUMERIC, BIGNUMERIC, and STRING, a type is picked if it is in the specified list and if it supports the precision and the scale. STRING supports all precision and scale values. If none of the listed types supports the precision and the scale, the type supporting the widest range in the specified list is picked, and if a value exceeds the supported range when reading the data, an error will be thrown. Example: Suppose the value of this field is ["NUMERIC", "BIGNUMERIC"]. If (precision,scale) is: * (38,9) -> NUMERIC; * (39,9) -> BIGNUMERIC (NUMERIC cannot hold 30 integer digits); * (38,10) -> BIGNUMERIC (NUMERIC cannot hold 10 fractional digits); * (76,38) -> BIGNUMERIC; * (77,38) -> BIGNUMERIC (error if value exceeds supported range). This field cannot contain duplicate types. The order of the types in this field is ignored. For example, ["BIGNUMERIC", "NUMERIC"] is the same as ["NUMERIC", "BIGNUMERIC"] and NUMERIC always takes precedence over BIGNUMERIC. Defaults to ["NUMERIC", "STRING"] for ORC and ["NUMERIC"] for the other file formats.

fields.name: ignoreUnknownValues
fields.description: Optional. Indicates if BigQuery should allow extra values that are not represented in the table schema. If true, the extra values are ignored. If false, records with extra columns are treated as bad records, and if there are too many bad records, an invalid error is returned in the job result. The default value is false. The sourceFormat property determines what BigQuery treats as an extra value: CSV: Trailing columns JSON: Named values that don't match any column names Google Cloud Bigtable: This setting is ignored. Google Cloud Datastore backups: This setting is ignored. Avro: This setting is ignored. ORC: This setting is ignored. Parquet: This setting is ignored.

fields.name: maxBadRecords
fields.description: Optional. The maximum number of bad records that BigQuery can ignore when reading data. If the number of bad records exceeds this value, an invalid error is returned in the job result. The default value is 0, which requires that all records are valid. This setting is ignored for Google Cloud Bigtable, Google Cloud Datastore backups, Avro, ORC and Parquet formats.

fields.name: referenceFileSchemaUri
fields.description: Optional. When creating an external table, the user can provide a reference file with the table schema. This is enabled for the following formats: AVRO, PARQUET, ORC.

fields.name: sourceFormat
fields.description: [Required] The data format. For CSV files, specify "CSV". For Google sheets, specify "GOOGLE_SHEETS". For newline-delimited JSON, specify "NEWLINE_DELIMITED_JSON". For Avro files, specify "AVRO". For Google Cloud Datastore backups, specify "DATASTORE_BACKUP". For Apache Iceberg tables, specify "ICEBERG". For ORC files, specify "ORC". For Parquet files, specify "PARQUET". [Beta] For Google Cloud Bigtable, specify "BIGTABLE".

fields.name: sourceUris
fields.description: [Required] The fully-qualified URIs that point to your data in Google Cloud. For Google Cloud Storage URIs: Each URI can contain one '*' wildcard character and it must come after the 'bucket' name. Size limits related to load jobs apply to external data sources. For Google Cloud Bigtable URIs: Exactly one URI can be specified and it has be a fully specified and valid HTTPS URL for a Google Cloud Bigtable table. For Google Cloud Datastore backups, exactly one URI can be specified. Also, the '*' wildcard character is not allowed.

fields.name: timeFormat
fields.description: Optional. Format used to parse TIME values. Supports C-style and SQL-style values.

fields.name: timeZone
fields.description: Optional. Time zone used when parsing timestamp values that do not have specific time zone information (e.g. 2024-04-20 12:34:56). The expected format is a IANA timezone string (e.g. America/Los_Angeles).

fields.name: timestampFormat
fields.description: Optional. Format used to parse TIMESTAMP values. Supports C-style and SQL-style values.

fields.name: timestampTargetPrecision
fields.description: Precisions (maximum number of total digits in base 10) for seconds of TIMESTAMP types that are allowed to the destination table for autodetection mode. Available for the formats: CSV, PARQUET, and AVRO. Possible values include: Not Specified, [], or [6]: timestamp(6) for all auto detected TIMESTAMP columns [6, 12]: timestamp(6) for all auto detected TIMESTAMP columns that have less than 6 digits of subseconds. timestamp(12) for all auto detected TIMESTAMP columns that have more than 6 digits of subseconds. [12]: timestamp(12) for all auto detected TIMESTAMP columns. The order of the elements in this array is ignored. Inputs that have higher precision than the highest target precision in this array will be truncated.

types.name: AvroOptions
types.description: Options for external data sources.

fields.name: useAvroLogicalTypes
fields.description: Optional. If sourceFormat is set to "AVRO", indicates whether to interpret logical types as the corresponding BigQuery data type (for example, TIMESTAMP), instead of using the raw type (for example, INTEGER).

types.name: BigtableOptions
types.description: Options specific to Google Cloud Bigtable data sources.

fields.name: columnFamilies
fields.description: Optional. List of column families to expose in the table schema along with their types. This list restricts the column families that can be referenced in queries and specifies their value types. You can use this list to do type conversions - see the 'type' field for more details. If you leave this list empty, all column families are present in the table schema and their values are read as BYTES. During a query only the column families referenced in that query are read from Bigtable.

fields.name: ignoreUnspecifiedColumnFamilies
fields.description: Optional. If field is true, then the column families that are not specified in columnFamilies list are not exposed in the table schema. Otherwise, they are read with BYTES type values. The default value is false.

fields.name: outputColumnFamiliesAsJson
fields.description: Optional. If field is true, then each column family will be read as a single JSON column. Otherwise they are read as a repeated cell structure containing timestamp/value tuples. The default value is false.

fields.name: readRowkeyAsString
fields.description: Optional. If field is true, then the rowkey column families will be read and converted to string. Otherwise they are read with BYTES type values and users need to manually cast them with CAST if necessary. The default value is false.

types.name: BigtableColumnFamily
types.description: Information related to a Bigtable column family.

fields.name: columns
fields.description: Optional. Lists of columns that should be exposed as individual fields as opposed to a list of (column name, value) pairs. All columns whose qualifier matches a qualifier in this list can be accessed as `.`. Other columns can be accessed as a list through the `.Column` field.

fields.name: encoding
fields.description: Optional. The encoding of the values when the type is not STRING. Acceptable encoding values are: TEXT - indicates values are alphanumeric text strings. BINARY - indicates values are encoded using HBase Bytes.toBytes family of functions. PROTO_BINARY - indicates values are encoded using serialized proto messages. This can only be used in combination with JSON type. This can be overridden for a specific column by listing that column in 'columns' and specifying an encoding for it.

fields.name: familyId
fields.description: Identifier of the column family.

fields.name: onlyReadLatest
fields.description: Optional. If this is set only the latest version of value are exposed for all columns in this column family. This can be overridden for a specific column by listing that column in 'columns' and specifying a different setting for that column.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Optional. The type to convert the value in cells of this column family. The values are expected to be encoded using HBase Bytes.toBytes function when using the BINARY encoding value. Following BigQuery types are allowed (case-sensitive): * BYTES * STRING * INTEGER * FLOAT * BOOLEAN * JSON Default type is BYTES. This can be overridden for a specific column by listing that column in 'columns' and specifying a type for it.

types.name: BigtableColumn
types.description: Information related to a Bigtable column.

fields.name: encoding
fields.description: Optional. The encoding of the values when the type is not STRING. Acceptable encoding values are: TEXT - indicates values are alphanumeric text strings. BINARY - indicates values are encoded using HBase Bytes.toBytes family of functions. PROTO_BINARY - indicates values are encoded using serialized proto messages. This can only be used in combination with JSON type. 'encoding' can also be set at the column family level. However, the setting at this level takes precedence if 'encoding' is set at both levels.

fields.name: fieldName
fields.description: Optional. If the qualifier is not a valid BigQuery field identifier i.e. does not match a-zA-Z*, a valid identifier must be provided as the column field name and is used as field name in queries.

fields.name: onlyReadLatest
fields.description: Optional. If this is set, only the latest version of value in this column are exposed. 'onlyReadLatest' can also be set at the column family level. However, the setting at this level takes precedence if 'onlyReadLatest' is set at both levels.

fields.name: qualifierEncoded
fields.description: [Required] Qualifier of the column. Columns in the parent column family that has this exact qualifier are exposed as `.` field. If the qualifier is valid UTF-8 string, it can be specified in the qualifier_string field. Otherwise, a base-64 encoded value must be set to qualifier_encoded. The column field name is the same as the column qualifier. However, if the qualifier is not a valid BigQuery field identifier i.e. does not match a-zA-Z*, a valid identifier must be provided as field_name.

fields.name: qualifierString
fields.description: Qualifier string.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Optional. The type to convert the value in cells of this column. The values are expected to be encoded using HBase Bytes.toBytes function when using the BINARY encoding value. Following BigQuery types are allowed (case-sensitive): * BYTES * STRING * INTEGER * FLOAT * BOOLEAN * JSON Default type is BYTES. 'type' can also be set at the column family level. However, the setting at this level takes precedence if 'type' is set at both levels.

types.name: BigtableProtoConfig
types.description: Information related to a Bigtable protobuf column.

fields.name: protoMessageName
fields.description: Optional. The fully qualified proto message name of the protobuf. In the format of "foo.bar.Message".

fields.name: schemaBundleId
fields.description: Optional. The ID of the Bigtable SchemaBundle resource associated with this protobuf. The ID should be referred to within the parent table, e.g., `foo` rather than `projects/{project}/instances/{instance}/tables/{table}/schemaBundles/foo`. See [more details on Bigtable SchemaBundles](https://docs.cloud.google.com/bigtable/docs/create-manage-protobuf-schemas).

types.name: Byte
types.description: The `Byte` scalar type represents byte value as a Buffer

types.name: CsvOptions
types.description: Information related to a CSV data source.

fields.name: allowJaggedRows
fields.description: Optional. Indicates if BigQuery should accept rows that are missing trailing optional columns. If true, BigQuery treats missing trailing columns as null values. If false, records with missing trailing columns are treated as bad records, and if there are too many bad records, an invalid error is returned in the job result. The default value is false.

fields.name: allowQuotedNewlines
fields.description: Optional. Indicates if BigQuery should allow quoted data sections that contain newline characters in a CSV file. The default value is false.

fields.name: encoding
fields.description: Optional. The character encoding of the data. The supported values are UTF-8, ISO-8859-1, UTF-16BE, UTF-16LE, UTF-32BE, and UTF-32LE. The default value is UTF-8. BigQuery decodes the data after the raw, binary data has been split using the values of the quote and fieldDelimiter properties.

fields.name: fieldDelimiter
fields.description: Optional. The separator character for fields in a CSV file. The separator is interpreted as a single byte. For files encoded in ISO-8859-1, any single character can be used as a separator. For files encoded in UTF-8, characters represented in decimal range 1-127 (U+0001-U+007F) can be used without any modification. UTF-8 characters encoded with multiple bytes (i.e. U+0080 and above) will have only the first byte used for separating fields. The remaining bytes will be treated as a part of the field. BigQuery also supports the escape sequence "\t" (U+0009) to specify a tab separator. The default value is comma (",", U+002C).

fields.name: nullMarker
fields.description: Optional. Specifies a string that represents a null value in a CSV file. For example, if you specify "\N", BigQuery interprets "\N" as a null value when querying a CSV file. The default value is the empty string. If you set this property to a custom value, BigQuery throws an error if an empty string is present for all data types except for STRING and BYTE. For STRING and BYTE columns, BigQuery interprets the empty string as an empty value.

fields.name: nullMarkers
fields.description: Optional. A list of strings represented as SQL NULL value in a CSV file. null_marker and null_markers can't be set at the same time. If null_marker is set, null_markers has to be not set. If null_markers is set, null_marker has to be not set. If both null_marker and null_markers are set at the same time, a user error would be thrown. Any strings listed in null_markers, including empty string would be interpreted as SQL NULL. This applies to all column types.

fields.name: preserveAsciiControlCharacters
fields.description: Optional. Indicates if the embedded ASCII control characters (the first 32 characters in the ASCII-table, from '\x00' to '\x1F') are preserved.

fields.name: skipLeadingRows
fields.description: Optional. The number of rows at the top of a CSV file that BigQuery will skip when reading the data. The default value is 0. This property is useful if you have header rows in the file that should be skipped. When autodetect is on, the behavior is the following: * skipLeadingRows unspecified - Autodetect tries to detect headers in the first row. If they are not detected, the row is read as data. Otherwise data is read starting from the second row. * skipLeadingRows is 0 - Instructs autodetect that there are no headers and data should be read starting from the first row. * skipLeadingRows = N > 0 - Autodetect skips N-1 rows and tries to detect headers in row N. If headers are not detected, row N is just skipped. Otherwise row N is used to extract column names for the detected schema.

fields.name: sourceColumnMatch
fields.description: Optional. Controls the strategy used to match loaded columns to the schema. If not set, a sensible default is chosen based on how the schema is provided. If autodetect is used, then columns are matched by name. Otherwise, columns are matched by position. This is done to keep the behavior backward-compatible. Acceptable values are: POSITION - matches by position. This assumes that the columns are ordered the same way as the schema. NAME - matches by name. This reads the header row as column names and reorders columns to match the field names in the schema.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_configuration_query_tableDefinitions_additionalProperties_fileSetSpecType
types.description: Optional. Specifies how source URIs are interpreted for constructing the file set to load. By default source URIs are expanded against the underlying storage. Other options include specifying manifest files. Only applicable to object storage systems.

types.name: GoogleSheetsOptions
types.description: Options specific to Google Sheets data sources.

fields.name: range
fields.description: Optional. Range of a sheet to query from. Only used when non-empty. Typical format: sheet_name!top_left_cell_id:bottom_right_cell_id For example: sheet1!A1:B20

fields.name: skipLeadingRows
fields.description: Optional. The number of rows at the top of a sheet that BigQuery will skip when reading the data. The default value is 0. This property is useful if you have header rows that should be skipped. When autodetect is on, the behavior is the following: * skipLeadingRows unspecified - Autodetect tries to detect headers in the first row. If they are not detected, the row is read as data. Otherwise data is read starting from the second row. * skipLeadingRows is 0 - Instructs autodetect that there are no headers and data should be read starting from the first row. * skipLeadingRows = N > 0 - Autodetect skips N-1 rows and tries to detect headers in row N. If headers are not detected, row N is just skipped. Otherwise row N is used to extract column names for the detected schema.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_configuration_query_tableDefinitions_additionalProperties_jsonExtension
types.description: Optional. Load option to be used together with source_format newline-delimited JSON to indicate that a variant of JSON is being loaded. To load newline-delimited GeoJSON, specify GEOJSON (and source_format must be set to NEWLINE_DELIMITED_JSON).

types.name: JsonOptions
types.description: Json Options for load and make external tables.

fields.name: encoding
fields.description: Optional. The character encoding of the data. The supported values are UTF-8, UTF-16BE, UTF-16LE, UTF-32BE, and UTF-32LE. The default value is UTF-8.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_configuration_query_tableDefinitions_additionalProperties_metadataCacheMode
types.description: Optional. Metadata Cache Mode for the table. Set this to enable caching of metadata from external data source.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_configuration_query_tableDefinitions_additionalProperties_objectMetadata
types.description: Optional. ObjectMetadata is used to create Object Tables. Object Tables contain a listing of objects (with their metadata) found at the source_uris. If ObjectMetadata is set, source_format should be omitted. Currently SIMPLE is the only supported Object Metadata type.

types.name: UserDefinedFunctionResource
types.description: This is used for defining User Defined Function (UDF) resources only when using legacy SQL. Users of GoogleSQL should leverage either DDL (e.g. CREATE [TEMPORARY] FUNCTION ... ) or the Routines API to define UDF resources. For additional information on migrating, see: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/standard-sql/migrating-from-legacy-sql#differences_in_user-defined_javascript_functions

fields.name: inlineCode
fields.description: [Pick one] An inline resource that contains code for a user-defined function (UDF). Providing a inline code resource is equivalent to providing a URI for a file containing the same code.

fields.name: resourceUri
fields.description: [Pick one] A code resource to load from a Google Cloud Storage URI (gs://bucket/path).

types.name: JobCreationReason
types.description: Reason about why a Job was created from a [`jobs.query`](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/rest/v2/jobs/query) method when used with `JOB_CREATION_OPTIONAL` Job creation mode. For [`jobs.insert`](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/rest/v2/jobs/insert) method calls it will always be `REQUESTED`.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_jobCreationReason_code
types.description: Output only. Specifies the high level reason why a Job was created.

types.name: JobReference
types.description: A job reference is a fully qualified identifier for referring to a job.

fields.name: jobId
fields.description: Required. The ID of the job. The ID must contain only letters (a-z, A-Z), numbers (0-9), underscores (_), or dashes (-). The maximum length is 1,024 characters.

fields.name: location
fields.description: Optional. The geographic location of the job. The default value is US. For more information about BigQuery locations, see: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/locations

fields.name: projectId
fields.description: Required. The ID of the project containing this job.

types.name: JobStatistics
types.description: Statistics for a single job execution.

fields.name: completionRatio
fields.description: Output only. [TrustedTester] Job progress (0.0 -> 1.0) for LOAD and EXTRACT jobs.

fields.name: creationTime
fields.description: Output only. Creation time of this job, in milliseconds since the epoch. This field will be present on all jobs.

fields.name: endTime
fields.description: Output only. End time of this job, in milliseconds since the epoch. This field will be present whenever a job is in the DONE state.

fields.name: finalExecutionDurationMs
fields.description: Output only. The duration in milliseconds of the execution of the final attempt of this job, as BigQuery may internally re-attempt to execute the job.

fields.name: numChildJobs
fields.description: Output only. Number of child jobs executed.

fields.name: parentJobId
fields.description: Output only. If this is a child job, specifies the job ID of the parent.

fields.name: quotaDeferments
fields.description: Output only. Quotas which delayed this job's start time.

fields.name: reservationGroupPath
fields.description: Output only. The reservation group path of the reservation assigned to this job. This field has a limit of 10 nested reservation groups. This is to maintain consistency between reservatins info schema and jobs info schema. The first reservation group is the root reservation group and the last is the leaf or lowest level reservation group.

fields.name: reservationUsage
fields.description: Output only. Job resource usage breakdown by reservation. This field reported misleading information and will no longer be populated.

fields.name: reservation_id
fields.description: Output only. Name of the primary reservation assigned to this job. Note that this could be different than reservations reported in the reservation usage field if parent reservations were used to execute this job.

fields.name: startTime
fields.description: Output only. Start time of this job, in milliseconds since the epoch. This field will be present when the job transitions from the PENDING state to either RUNNING or DONE.

fields.name: totalBytesProcessed
fields.description: Output only. Total bytes processed for the job.

fields.name: totalSlotMs
fields.description: Output only. Slot-milliseconds for the job.

types.name: Float
types.description: The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by [IEEE 754](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

types.name: JobStatistics5
types.description: Statistics for a copy job.

fields.name: copiedLogicalBytes
fields.description: Output only. Number of logical bytes copied to the destination table.

fields.name: copiedRows
fields.description: Output only. Number of rows copied to the destination table.

types.name: DataMaskingStatistics
types.description: Statistics for data-masking.

fields.name: dataMaskingApplied
fields.description: Whether any accessed data was protected by the data masking.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_edition
types.description: Output only. Name of edition corresponding to the reservation for this job at the time of this update.

types.name: JobStatistics4
types.description: Statistics for an extract job.

fields.name: destinationUriFileCounts
fields.description: Output only. Number of files per destination URI or URI pattern specified in the extract configuration. These values will be in the same order as the URIs specified in the 'destinationUris' field.

fields.name: inputBytes
fields.description: Output only. Number of user bytes extracted into the result. This is the byte count as computed by BigQuery for billing purposes and doesn't have any relationship with the number of actual result bytes extracted in the desired format.

fields.name: timeline
fields.description: Output only. Describes a timeline of job execution.

types.name: QueryTimelineSample
types.description: Summary of the state of query execution at a given time.

fields.name: activeUnits
fields.description: Total number of active workers. This does not correspond directly to slot usage. This is the largest value observed since the last sample.

fields.name: completedUnits
fields.description: Total parallel units of work completed by this query.

fields.name: elapsedMs
fields.description: Milliseconds elapsed since the start of query execution.

fields.name: estimatedRunnableUnits
fields.description: Units of work that can be scheduled immediately. Providing additional slots for these units of work will accelerate the query, if no other query in the reservation needs additional slots.

fields.name: pendingUnits
fields.description: Total units of work remaining for the query. This number can be revised (increased or decreased) while the query is running.

fields.name: shuffleRamUsageRatio
fields.description: Total shuffle usage ratio in shuffle RAM per reservation of this query. This will be provided for reservation customers only.

fields.name: totalSlotMs
fields.description: Cumulative slot-ms consumed by the query.

types.name: JobStatistics3
types.description: Statistics for a load job.

fields.name: badRecords
fields.description: Output only. The number of bad records encountered. Note that if the job has failed because of more bad records encountered than the maximum allowed in the load job configuration, then this number can be less than the total number of bad records present in the input data.

fields.name: inputFileBytes
fields.description: Output only. Number of bytes of source data in a load job.

fields.name: inputFiles
fields.description: Output only. Number of source files in a load job.

fields.name: outputBytes
fields.description: Output only. Size of the loaded data in bytes. Note that while a load job is in the running state, this value may change.

fields.name: outputRows
fields.description: Output only. Number of rows imported in a load job. Note that while an import job is in the running state, this value may change.

fields.name: timeline
fields.description: Output only. Describes a timeline of job execution.

types.name: JobStatistics2
types.description: Statistics for a query job.

fields.name: billingTier
fields.description: Output only. Billing tier for the job. This is a BigQuery-specific concept which is not related to the Google Cloud notion of "free tier". The value here is a measure of the query's resource consumption relative to the amount of data scanned. For on-demand queries, the limit is 100, and all queries within this limit are billed at the standard on-demand rates. On-demand queries that exceed this limit will fail with a billingTierLimitExceeded error.

fields.name: cacheHit
fields.description: Output only. Whether the query result was fetched from the query cache.

fields.name: ddlAffectedRowAccessPolicyCount
fields.description: Output only. The number of row access policies affected by a DDL statement. Present only for DROP ALL ROW ACCESS POLICIES queries.

fields.name: ddlOperationPerformed
fields.description: Output only. The DDL operation performed, possibly dependent on the pre-existence of the DDL target.

fields.name: estimatedBytesProcessed
fields.description: Output only. The original estimate of bytes processed for the job.

fields.name: externalServiceCosts
fields.description: Output only. Job cost breakdown as bigquery internal cost and external service costs.

fields.name: modelTrainingCurrentIteration
fields.description: Deprecated.

fields.name: modelTrainingExpectedTotalIteration
fields.description: Deprecated.

fields.name: numDmlAffectedRows
fields.description: Output only. The number of rows affected by a DML statement. Present only for DML statements INSERT, UPDATE or DELETE.

fields.name: queryPlan
fields.description: Output only. Describes execution plan for the query.

fields.name: referencedPropertyGraphs
fields.description: Output only. Referenced property graphs for the job. Queries that reference more than 50 property graphs will not have a complete list.

fields.name: referencedRoutines
fields.description: Output only. Referenced routines for the job.

fields.name: referencedTables
fields.description: Output only. Referenced tables for the job.

fields.name: reservationUsage
fields.description: Output only. Job resource usage breakdown by reservation. This field reported misleading information and will no longer be populated.

fields.name: statementType

Possible values: * `SELECT`: [`SELECT`](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/standard-sql/query-syntax#select_list) statement. * `ASSERT`: fields.name: timeline
fields.description: Output only. Describes a timeline of job execution.

fields.name: totalBytesBilled
fields.description: Output only. If the project is configured to use on-demand pricing, then this field contains the total bytes billed for the job. If the project is configured to use flat-rate pricing, then you are not billed for bytes and this field is informational only.

fields.name: totalBytesProcessed
fields.description: Output only. Total bytes processed for the job.

fields.name: totalBytesProcessedAccuracy
fields.description: Output only. For dry-run jobs, totalBytesProcessed is an estimate and this field specifies the accuracy of the estimate. Possible values can be: UNKNOWN: accuracy of the estimate is unknown. PRECISE: estimate is precise. LOWER_BOUND: estimate is lower bound of what the query would cost. UPPER_BOUND: estimate is upper bound of what the query would cost.

fields.name: totalPartitionsProcessed
fields.description: Output only. Total number of partitions processed from all partitioned tables referenced in the job.

fields.name: totalServicesSkuSlotMs
fields.description: Output only. Total slot milliseconds for the job that ran on external services and billed on the services SKU. This field is only populated for jobs that have external service costs, and is the total of the usage for costs whose billing method is `"SERVICES_SKU"`.

fields.name: totalSlotMs
fields.description: Output only. Slot-milliseconds for the job.

fields.name: transferredBytes
fields.description: Output only. Total bytes transferred for cross-cloud queries such as Cross Cloud Transfer and CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS).

fields.name: undeclaredQueryParameters
fields.description: Output only. GoogleSQL only: list of undeclared query parameters detected during a dry run validation.

types.name: BiEngineStatistics
types.description: Statistics for a BI Engine specific query. Populated as part of JobStatistics2

fields.name: biEngineReasons
fields.description: In case of DISABLED or PARTIAL bi_engine_mode, these contain the explanatory reasons as to why BI Engine could not accelerate. In case the full query was accelerated, this field is not populated.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_biEngineStatistics_accelerationMode
types.description: Output only. Specifies which mode of BI Engine acceleration was performed (if any).

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_biEngineStatistics_biEngineMode
types.description: Output only. Specifies which mode of BI Engine acceleration was performed (if any).

types.name: BiEngineReason
types.description: Reason why BI Engine didn't accelerate the query (or sub-query).

fields.name: message
fields.description: Output only. Free form human-readable reason for partial or disabled acceleration.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_biEngineStatistics_biEngineReasons_items_code
types.description: Output only. High-level BI Engine reason for partial or disabled acceleration

types.name: RowAccessPolicyReference
types.description: Id path of a row access policy.

fields.name: datasetId
fields.description: Required. The ID of the dataset containing this row access policy.

fields.name: policyId
fields.description: Required. The ID of the row access policy. The ID must contain only letters (a-z, A-Z), numbers (0-9), or underscores (_). The maximum length is 256 characters.

fields.name: projectId
fields.description: Required. The ID of the project containing this row access policy.

fields.name: tableId
fields.description: Required. The ID of the table containing this row access policy.

types.name: DmlStatistics
types.description: Detailed statistics for DML statements

fields.name: deletedRowCount
fields.description: Output only. Number of deleted Rows. populated by DML DELETE, MERGE and TRUNCATE statements.

fields.name: insertedRowCount
fields.description: Output only. Number of inserted Rows. Populated by DML INSERT and MERGE statements

fields.name: updatedRowCount
fields.description: Output only. Number of updated Rows. Populated by DML UPDATE and MERGE statements.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_dmlStats_dmlMode
types.description: Output only. DML mode used.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_dmlStats_fineGrainedDmlUnusedReason
types.description: Output only. Reason for disabling fine-grained DML if applicable.

types.name: ExportDataStatistics
types.description: Statistics for the EXPORT DATA statement as part of Query Job. EXTRACT JOB statistics are populated in JobStatistics4.

fields.name: fileCount
fields.description: Number of destination files generated in case of EXPORT DATA statement only.

fields.name: rowCount
fields.description: [Alpha] Number of destination rows generated in case of EXPORT DATA statement only.

types.name: ExternalServiceCost
types.description: The external service cost is a portion of the total cost, these costs are not additive with total_bytes_billed. Moreover, this field only track external service costs that will show up as BigQuery costs (e.g. training BigQuery ML job with google cloud CAIP or Automl Tables services), not other costs which may be accrued by running the query (e.g. reading from Bigtable or Cloud Storage). The external service costs with different billing sku (e.g. CAIP job is charged based on VM usage) are converted to BigQuery billed_bytes and slot_ms with equivalent amount of US dollars. Services may not directly correlate to these metrics, but these are the equivalents for billing purposes. Output only.

fields.name: billingMethod
fields.description: The billing method used for the external job. This field, set to `SERVICES_SKU`, is only used when billing under the services SKU. Otherwise, it is unspecified for backward compatibility.

fields.name: bytesBilled
fields.description: External service cost in terms of bigquery bytes billed.

fields.name: bytesProcessed
fields.description: External service cost in terms of bigquery bytes processed.

fields.name: externalService
fields.description: External service name.

fields.name: reservedSlotCount
fields.description: Non-preemptable reserved slots used for external job. For example, reserved slots for Cloua AI Platform job are the VM usages converted to BigQuery slot with equivalent mount of price.

fields.name: slotMs
fields.description: External service cost in terms of bigquery slot milliseconds.

types.name: GenAiStats
types.description: GenAi stats for the query job.

fields.name: functionStats
fields.description: Function level stats for GenAi Functions. See https://docs.cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/generative-ai-overview

types.name: GenAiErrorStats
types.description: Provides error statistics for the query job across all AI function calls.

fields.name: errors
fields.description: A list of unique errors at query level (up to 5, truncated to 100 chars)

types.name: GenAiFunctionStats
types.description: Provides statistics for each Ai function call within a query.

fields.name: functionName
fields.description: Name of the function.

fields.name: numProcessedRows
fields.description: Number of rows processed by this GenAi function. This includes all cost_optimized, llm_inferred and failed_rows.

fields.name: prompt
fields.description: User input prompt of the function (truncated to 20 chars).

types.name: GenAiFunctionCostOptimizationStats
types.description: Provides cost optimization statistics for a GenAi function call.

fields.name: message
fields.description: System generated message to provide insights into cost optimization state.

fields.name: numCostOptimizedRows
fields.description: Number of rows inferred via cost optimized workflow.

types.name: GenAiFunctionErrorStats
types.description: Provides error statistics for a GenAi function call.

fields.name: errors
fields.description: A list of unique errors at function level (up to 5, truncated to 100 chars).

fields.name: numFailedRows
fields.description: Number of failed rows processed by the function

types.name: IncrementalResultStats
types.description: Statistics related to Incremental Query Results. Populated as part of JobStatistics2. This feature is not yet available.

fields.name: disabledReasonDetails
fields.description: Output only. Additional human-readable clarification, if available, for DisabledReason.

fields.name: firstIncrementalRowTime
fields.description: Output only. The time at which the first incremental result was written. If the query needed to restart internally, this only describes the final attempt.

fields.name: incrementalRowCount
fields.description: Output only. Number of rows that were in the latest result set before query completion.

fields.name: lastIncrementalRowTime
fields.description: Output only. The time at which the last incremental result was written. Does not include the final result written after query completion.

fields.name: resultSetLastModifyTime
fields.description: Output only. The time at which the result table's contents were modified. May be absent if no results have been written or the query has completed.

fields.name: resultSetLastReplaceTime
fields.description: Output only. The time at which the result table's contents were completely replaced. May be absent if no results have been written or the query has completed.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_incrementalResultStats_disabledReason
types.description: Output only. Reason why incremental query results are/were not written by the query.

types.name: LoadQueryStatistics
types.description: Statistics for a LOAD query.

fields.name: badRecords
fields.description: Output only. The number of bad records encountered while processing a LOAD query. Note that if the job has failed because of more bad records encountered than the maximum allowed in the load job configuration, then this number can be less than the total number of bad records present in the input data.

fields.name: bytesTransferred
fields.description: Output only. This field is deprecated. The number of bytes of source data copied over the network for a `LOAD` query. `transferred_bytes` has the canonical value for physical transferred bytes, which is used for BigQuery Omni billing.

fields.name: inputFileBytes
fields.description: Output only. Number of bytes of source data in a LOAD query.

fields.name: inputFiles
fields.description: Output only. Number of source files in a LOAD query.

fields.name: outputBytes
fields.description: Output only. Size of the loaded data in bytes. Note that while a LOAD query is in the running state, this value may change.

fields.name: outputRows
fields.description: Output only. Number of rows imported in a LOAD query. Note that while a LOAD query is in the running state, this value may change.

types.name: MaterializedViewStatistics
types.description: Statistics of materialized views considered in a query job.

fields.name: materializedView
fields.description: Materialized views considered for the query job. Only certain materialized views are used. For a detailed list, see the child message. If many materialized views are considered, then the list might be incomplete.

types.name: MaterializedView
types.description: A materialized view considered for a query job.

fields.name: chosen
fields.description: Whether the materialized view is chosen for the query. A materialized view can be chosen to rewrite multiple parts of the same query. If a materialized view is chosen to rewrite any part of the query, then this field is true, even if the materialized view was not chosen to rewrite others parts.

fields.name: estimatedBytesSaved
fields.description: If present, specifies a best-effort estimation of the bytes saved by using the materialized view rather than its base tables.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_materializedViewStatistics_materializedView_items_rejectedReason
types.description: If present, specifies the reason why the materialized view was not chosen for the query.

types.name: MetadataCacheStatistics
types.description: Statistics for metadata caching in queried tables.

fields.name: tableMetadataCacheUsage
fields.description: Set for the Metadata caching eligible tables referenced in the query.

types.name: TableMetadataCacheUsage
types.description: Table level detail on the usage of metadata caching. Only set for Metadata caching eligible tables referenced in the query.

fields.name: explanation
fields.description: Free form human-readable reason metadata caching was unused for the job.

fields.name: staleness
fields.description: Duration since last refresh as of this job for managed tables (indicates metadata cache staleness as seen by this job).

fields.name: tableType
fields.description: [Table type](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/rest/v2/tables#Table.FIELDS.type).

types.name: PruningStats
types.description: The column metadata index pruning statistics.

fields.name: postCmetaPruningParallelInputCount
fields.description: The number of parallel inputs matched.

fields.name: postCmetaPruningPartitionCount
fields.description: The number of partitions matched.

fields.name: preCmetaPruningParallelInputCount
fields.description: The number of parallel inputs scanned.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_metadataCacheStatistics_tableMetadataCacheUsage_items_unusedReason
types.description: Reason for not using metadata caching for the table.

types.name: MlStatistics
types.description: Job statistics specific to a BigQuery ML training job.

fields.name: hparamTrials
fields.description: Output only. Trials of a [hyperparameter tuning job](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery-ml/docs/reference/standard-sql/bigqueryml-syntax-hp-tuning-overview) sorted by trial_id.

fields.name: iterationResults
fields.description: Results for all completed iterations. Empty for [hyperparameter tuning jobs](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery-ml/docs/reference/standard-sql/bigqueryml-syntax-hp-tuning-overview).

fields.name: maxIterations
fields.description: Output only. Maximum number of iterations specified as max_iterations in the 'CREATE MODEL' query. The actual number of iterations may be less than this number due to early stop.

types.name: HparamTuningTrial
types.description: Training info of a trial in [hyperparameter tuning](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery-ml/docs/reference/standard-sql/bigqueryml-syntax-hp-tuning-overview) models.

fields.name: endTimeMs
fields.description: Ending time of the trial.

fields.name: errorMessage
fields.description: Error message for FAILED and INFEASIBLE trial.

fields.name: evalLoss
fields.description: Loss computed on the eval data at the end of trial.

fields.name: startTimeMs
fields.description: Starting time of the trial.

fields.name: trainingLoss
fields.description: Loss computed on the training data at the end of trial.

fields.name: trialId
fields.description: 1-based index of the trial.

types.name: EvaluationMetrics
types.description: Evaluation metrics of a model. These are either computed on all training data or just the eval data based on whether eval data was used during training. These are not present for imported models.

types.name: ArimaForecastingMetrics
types.description: Model evaluation metrics for ARIMA forecasting models.

fields.name: arimaFittingMetrics
fields.description: Arima model fitting metrics.

fields.name: arimaSingleModelForecastingMetrics
fields.description: Repeated as there can be many metric sets (one for each model) in auto-arima and the large-scale case.

fields.name: hasDrift
fields.description: Whether Arima model fitted with drift or not. It is always false when d is not 1.

fields.name: nonSeasonalOrder
fields.description: Non-seasonal order.

fields.name: seasonalPeriods
fields.description: Seasonal periods. Repeated because multiple periods are supported for one time series.

fields.name: timeSeriesId
fields.description: Id to differentiate different time series for the large-scale case.

types.name: ArimaFittingMetrics
types.description: ARIMA model fitting metrics.

fields.name: aic
fields.description: AIC.

fields.name: logLikelihood
fields.description: Log-likelihood.

fields.name: variance
fields.description: Variance.

types.name: ArimaSingleModelForecastingMetrics
types.description: Model evaluation metrics for a single ARIMA forecasting model.

fields.name: hasDrift
fields.description: Is arima model fitted with drift or not. It is always false when d is not 1.

fields.name: hasHolidayEffect
fields.description: If true, holiday_effect is a part of time series decomposition result.

fields.name: hasSpikesAndDips
fields.description: If true, spikes_and_dips is a part of time series decomposition result.

fields.name: hasStepChanges
fields.description: If true, step_changes is a part of time series decomposition result.

fields.name: seasonalPeriods
fields.description: Seasonal periods. Repeated because multiple periods are supported for one time series.

fields.name: timeSeriesId
fields.description: The time_series_id value for this time series. It will be one of the unique values from the time_series_id_column specified during ARIMA model training. Only present when time_series_id_column training option was used.

fields.name: timeSeriesIds
fields.description: The tuple of time_series_ids identifying this time series. It will be one of the unique tuples of values present in the time_series_id_columns specified during ARIMA model training. Only present when time_series_id_columns training option was used and the order of values here are same as the order of time_series_id_columns.

types.name: ArimaOrder
types.description: Arima order, can be used for both non-seasonal and seasonal parts.

fields.name: d
fields.description: Order of the differencing part.

fields.name: p
fields.description: Order of the autoregressive part.

fields.name: q
fields.description: Order of the moving-average part.

types.name: BinaryClassificationMetrics
types.description: Evaluation metrics for binary classification/classifier models.

fields.name: binaryConfusionMatrixList
fields.description: Binary confusion matrix at multiple thresholds.

fields.name: negativeLabel
fields.description: Label representing the negative class.

fields.name: positiveLabel
fields.description: Label representing the positive class.

types.name: AggregateClassificationMetrics
types.description: Aggregate metrics for classification/classifier models. For multi-class models, the metrics are either macro-averaged or micro-averaged. When macro-averaged, the metrics are calculated for each label and then an unweighted average is taken of those values. When micro-averaged, the metric is calculated globally by counting the total number of correctly predicted rows.

fields.name: accuracy
fields.description: Accuracy is the fraction of predictions given the correct label. For multiclass this is a micro-averaged metric.

fields.name: f1Score
fields.description: The F1 score is an average of recall and precision. For multiclass this is a macro-averaged metric.

fields.name: logLoss
fields.description: Logarithmic Loss. For multiclass this is a macro-averaged metric.

fields.name: precision
fields.description: Precision is the fraction of actual positive predictions that had positive actual labels. For multiclass this is a macro-averaged metric treating each class as a binary classifier.

fields.name: recall
fields.description: Recall is the fraction of actual positive labels that were given a positive prediction. For multiclass this is a macro-averaged metric.

fields.name: rocAuc
fields.description: Area Under a ROC Curve. For multiclass this is a macro-averaged metric.

fields.name: threshold
fields.description: Threshold at which the metrics are computed. For binary classification models this is the positive class threshold. For multi-class classification models this is the confidence threshold.

types.name: BinaryConfusionMatrix
types.description: Confusion matrix for binary classification models.

fields.name: accuracy
fields.description: The fraction of predictions given the correct label.

fields.name: f1Score
fields.description: The equally weighted average of recall and precision.

fields.name: falseNegatives
fields.description: Number of false samples predicted as false.

fields.name: falsePositives
fields.description: Number of false samples predicted as true.

fields.name: positiveClassThreshold
fields.description: Threshold value used when computing each of the following metric.

fields.name: precision
fields.description: The fraction of actual positive predictions that had positive actual labels.

fields.name: recall
fields.description: The fraction of actual positive labels that were given a positive prediction.

fields.name: trueNegatives
fields.description: Number of true samples predicted as false.

fields.name: truePositives
fields.description: Number of true samples predicted as true.

types.name: ClusteringMetrics
types.description: Evaluation metrics for clustering models.

fields.name: clusters
fields.description: Information for all clusters.

fields.name: daviesBouldinIndex
fields.description: Davies-Bouldin index.

fields.name: meanSquaredDistance
fields.description: Mean of squared distances between each sample to its cluster centroid.

types.name: Cluster
types.description: Message containing the information about one cluster.

fields.name: centroidId
fields.description: Centroid id.

fields.name: count
fields.description: Count of training data rows that were assigned to this cluster.

fields.name: featureValues
fields.description: Values of highly variant features for this cluster.

types.name: FeatureValue
types.description: Representative value of a single feature within the cluster.

fields.name: featureColumn
fields.description: The feature column name.

fields.name: numericalValue
fields.description: The numerical feature value. This is the centroid value for this feature.

types.name: CategoricalValue
types.description: Representative value of a categorical feature.

fields.name: categoryCounts
fields.description: Counts of all categories for the categorical feature. If there are more than ten categories, we return top ten (by count) and return one more CategoryCount with category "_OTHER_" and count as aggregate counts of remaining categories.

types.name: CategoryCount
types.description: Represents the count of a single category within the cluster.

fields.name: category
fields.description: The name of category.

fields.name: count
fields.description: The count of training samples matching the category within the cluster.

types.name: DimensionalityReductionMetrics
types.description: Model evaluation metrics for dimensionality reduction models.

fields.name: totalExplainedVarianceRatio
fields.description: Total percentage of variance explained by the selected principal components.

types.name: MultiClassClassificationMetrics
types.description: Evaluation metrics for multi-class classification/classifier models.

fields.name: confusionMatrixList
fields.description: Confusion matrix at different thresholds.

types.name: ConfusionMatrix
types.description: Confusion matrix for multi-class classification models.

fields.name: confidenceThreshold
fields.description: Confidence threshold used when computing the entries of the confusion matrix.

fields.name: rows
fields.description: One row per actual label.

types.name: Row
types.description: A single row in the confusion matrix.

fields.name: actualLabel
fields.description: The original label of this row.

fields.name: entries
fields.description: Info describing predicted label distribution.

types.name: Entry
types.description: A single entry in the confusion matrix.

fields.name: itemCount
fields.description: Number of items being predicted as this label.

fields.name: predictedLabel
fields.description: The predicted label. For confidence_threshold > 0, we will also add an entry indicating the number of items under the confidence threshold.

types.name: RankingMetrics
types.description: Evaluation metrics used by weighted-ALS models specified by feedback_type=implicit.

fields.name: averageRank
fields.description: Determines the goodness of a ranking by computing the percentile rank from the predicted confidence and dividing it by the original rank.

fields.name: meanAveragePrecision
fields.description: Calculates a precision per user for all the items by ranking them and then averages all the precisions across all the users.

fields.name: meanSquaredError
fields.description: Similar to the mean squared error computed in regression and explicit recommendation models except instead of computing the rating directly, the output from evaluate is computed against a preference which is 1 or 0 depending on if the rating exists or not.

fields.name: normalizedDiscountedCumulativeGain
fields.description: A metric to determine the goodness of a ranking calculated from the predicted confidence by comparing it to an ideal rank measured by the original ratings.

types.name: RegressionMetrics
types.description: Evaluation metrics for regression and explicit feedback type matrix factorization models.

fields.name: meanAbsoluteError
fields.description: Mean absolute error.

fields.name: meanSquaredError
fields.description: Mean squared error.

fields.name: meanSquaredLogError
fields.description: Mean squared log error.

fields.name: medianAbsoluteError
fields.description: Median absolute error.

fields.name: rSquared
fields.description: R^2 score. This corresponds to r2_score in ML.EVALUATE.

types.name: TrainingOptions
types.description: Options used in model training.

fields.name: activationFn
fields.description: Activation function of the neural nets.

fields.name: adjustStepChanges
fields.description: If true, detect step changes and make data adjustment in the input time series.

fields.name: approxGlobalFeatureContrib
fields.description: Whether to use approximate feature contribution method in XGBoost model explanation for global explain.

fields.name: autoArima
fields.description: Whether to enable auto ARIMA or not.

fields.name: autoArimaMaxOrder
fields.description: The max value of the sum of non-seasonal p and q.

fields.name: autoArimaMinOrder
fields.description: The min value of the sum of non-seasonal p and q.

fields.name: autoClassWeights
fields.description: Whether to calculate class weights automatically based on the popularity of each label.

fields.name: batchSize
fields.description: Batch size for dnn models.

fields.name: budgetHours
fields.description: Budget in hours for AutoML training.

fields.name: calculatePValues
fields.description: Whether or not p-value test should be computed for this model. Only available for linear and logistic regression models.

fields.name: cleanSpikesAndDips
fields.description: If true, clean spikes and dips in the input time series.

fields.name: colsampleBylevel
fields.description: Subsample ratio of columns for each level for boosted tree models.

fields.name: colsampleBynode
fields.description: Subsample ratio of columns for each node(split) for boosted tree models.

fields.name: colsampleBytree
fields.description: Subsample ratio of columns when constructing each tree for boosted tree models.

fields.name: contributionMetric
fields.description: The contribution metric. Applies to contribution analysis models. Allowed formats supported are for summable and summable ratio contribution metrics. These include expressions such as `SUM(x)` or `SUM(x)/SUM(y)`, where x and y are column names from the base table.

fields.name: dataSplitColumn
fields.description: The column to split data with. This column won't be used as a feature. 1. When data_split_method is CUSTOM, the corresponding column should be boolean. The rows with true value tag are eval data, and the false are training data. 2. When data_split_method is SEQ, the first DATA_SPLIT_EVAL_FRACTION rows (from smallest to largest) in the corresponding column are used as training data, and the rest are eval data. It respects the order in Orderable data types: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/standard-sql/data-types#data_type_properties

fields.name: dataSplitEvalFraction
fields.description: The fraction of evaluation data over the whole input data. The rest of data will be used as training data. The format should be double. Accurate to two decimal places. Default value is 0.2.

fields.name: decomposeTimeSeries
fields.description: If true, perform decompose time series and save the results.

fields.name: dimensionIdColumns
fields.description: Optional. Names of the columns to slice on. Applies to contribution analysis models.

fields.name: dropout
fields.description: Dropout probability for dnn models.

fields.name: earlyStop
fields.description: Whether to stop early when the loss doesn't improve significantly any more (compared to min_relative_progress). Used only for iterative training algorithms.

fields.name: enableGlobalExplain
fields.description: If true, enable global explanation during training.

fields.name: endpointIdleTtl
fields.description: The idle TTL of the endpoint before the resources get destroyed. The default value is 6.5 hours.

fields.name: fitIntercept
fields.description: Whether the model should include intercept during model training.

fields.name: forecastLimitLowerBound
fields.description: The forecast limit lower bound that was used during ARIMA model training with limits. To see more details of the algorithm: https://otexts.com/fpp2/limits.html

fields.name: forecastLimitUpperBound
fields.description: The forecast limit upper bound that was used during ARIMA model training with limits.

fields.name: hiddenUnits
fields.description: Hidden units for dnn models.

fields.name: holidayRegions
fields.description: A list of geographical regions that are used for time series modeling.

fields.name: horizon
fields.description: The number of periods ahead that need to be forecasted.

fields.name: hparamTuningObjectives
fields.description: The target evaluation metrics to optimize the hyperparameters for.

fields.name: huggingFaceModelId
fields.description: The id of a Hugging Face model. For example, `google/gemma-2-2b-it`.

fields.name: includeDrift
fields.description: Include drift when fitting an ARIMA model.

fields.name: initialLearnRate
fields.description: Specifies the initial learning rate for the line search learn rate strategy.

fields.name: inputLabelColumns
fields.description: Name of input label columns in training data.

fields.name: instanceWeightColumn
fields.description: Name of the instance weight column for training data. This column isn't be used as a feature.

fields.name: integratedGradientsNumSteps
fields.description: Number of integral steps for the integrated gradients explain method.

fields.name: isTestColumn
fields.description: Name of the column used to determine the rows corresponding to control and test. Applies to contribution analysis models.

fields.name: itemColumn
fields.description: Item column specified for matrix factorization models.

fields.name: kmeansInitializationColumn
fields.description: The column used to provide the initial centroids for kmeans algorithm when kmeans_initialization_method is CUSTOM.

fields.name: l1RegActivation
fields.description: L1 regularization coefficient to activations.

fields.name: l1Regularization
fields.description: L1 regularization coefficient.

fields.name: l2Regularization
fields.description: L2 regularization coefficient.

fields.name: labelClassWeights
fields.description: Weights associated with each label class, for rebalancing the training data. Only applicable for classification models.

fields.name: learnRate
fields.description: Learning rate in training. Used only for iterative training algorithms.

fields.name: machineType
fields.description: The type of the machine used to deploy and serve the model.

fields.name: maxIterations
fields.description: The maximum number of iterations in training. Used only for iterative training algorithms.

fields.name: maxParallelTrials
fields.description: Maximum number of trials to run in parallel.

fields.name: maxReplicaCount
fields.description: The maximum number of machine replicas that will be deployed on an endpoint. The default value is equal to min_replica_count.

fields.name: maxTimeSeriesLength
fields.description: The maximum number of time points in a time series that can be used in modeling the trend component of the time series. Don't use this option with the `timeSeriesLengthFraction` or `minTimeSeriesLength` options.

fields.name: maxTreeDepth
fields.description: Maximum depth of a tree for boosted tree models.

fields.name: minAprioriSupport
fields.description: The apriori support minimum. Applies to contribution analysis models.

fields.name: minRelativeProgress
fields.description: When early_stop is true, stops training when accuracy improvement is less than 'min_relative_progress'. Used only for iterative training algorithms.

fields.name: minReplicaCount
fields.description: The minimum number of machine replicas that will be always deployed on an endpoint. This value must be greater than or equal to 1. The default value is 1.

fields.name: minSplitLoss
fields.description: Minimum split loss for boosted tree models.

fields.name: minTimeSeriesLength
fields.description: The minimum number of time points in a time series that are used in modeling the trend component of the time series. If you use this option you must also set the `timeSeriesLengthFraction` option. This training option ensures that enough time points are available when you use `timeSeriesLengthFraction` in trend modeling. This is particularly important when forecasting multiple time series in a single query using `timeSeriesIdColumn`. If the total number of time points is less than the `minTimeSeriesLength` value, then the query uses all available time points.

fields.name: minTreeChildWeight
fields.description: Minimum sum of instance weight needed in a child for boosted tree models.

fields.name: modelGardenModelName
fields.description: The name of a Vertex model garden publisher model. Format is `publishers/{publisher}/models/{model}@{optional_version_id}`.

fields.name: modelUri
fields.description: Google Cloud Storage URI from which the model was imported. Only applicable for imported models.

fields.name: numClusters
fields.description: Number of clusters for clustering models.

fields.name: numFactors
fields.description: Num factors specified for matrix factorization models.

fields.name: numParallelTree
fields.description: Number of parallel trees constructed during each iteration for boosted tree models.

fields.name: numPrincipalComponents
fields.description: Number of principal components to keep in the PCA model. Must be <= the number of features.

fields.name: numTrials
fields.description: Number of trials to run this hyperparameter tuning job.

fields.name: optimizer
fields.description: Optimizer used for training the neural nets.

fields.name: pcaExplainedVarianceRatio
fields.description: The minimum ratio of cumulative explained variance that needs to be given by the PCA model.

fields.name: reservationAffinityKey
fields.description: Corresponds to the label key of a reservation resource used by Vertex AI. To target a SPECIFIC_RESERVATION by name, use `compute.googleapis.com/reservation-name` as the key and specify the name of your reservation as its value.

fields.name: reservationAffinityValues
fields.description: Corresponds to the label values of a reservation resource used by Vertex AI. This must be the full resource name of the reservation or reservation block.

fields.name: sampledShapleyNumPaths
fields.description: Number of paths for the sampled Shapley explain method.

fields.name: scaleFeatures
fields.description: If true, scale the feature values by dividing the feature standard deviation. Currently only apply to PCA.

fields.name: standardizeFeatures
fields.description: Whether to standardize numerical features. Default to true.

fields.name: subsample
fields.description: Subsample fraction of the training data to grow tree to prevent overfitting for boosted tree models.

fields.name: tfVersion
fields.description: Based on the selected TF version, the corresponding docker image is used to train external models.

fields.name: timeSeriesDataColumn
fields.description: Column to be designated as time series data for ARIMA model.

fields.name: timeSeriesIdColumn
fields.description: The time series id column that was used during ARIMA model training.

fields.name: timeSeriesIdColumns
fields.description: The time series id columns that were used during ARIMA model training.

fields.name: timeSeriesLengthFraction
fields.description: The fraction of the interpolated length of the time series that's used to model the time series trend component. All of the time points of the time series are used to model the non-trend component. This training option accelerates modeling training without sacrificing much forecasting accuracy. You can use this option with `minTimeSeriesLength` but not with `maxTimeSeriesLength`.

fields.name: timeSeriesTimestampColumn
fields.description: Column to be designated as time series timestamp for ARIMA model.

fields.name: trendSmoothingWindowSize
fields.description: Smoothing window size for the trend component. When a positive value is specified, a center moving average smoothing is applied on the history trend. When the smoothing window is out of the boundary at the beginning or the end of the trend, the first element or the last element is padded to fill the smoothing window before the average is applied.

fields.name: userColumn
fields.description: User column specified for matrix factorization models.

fields.name: vertexAiModelVersionAliases
fields.description: The version aliases to apply in Vertex AI model registry. Always overwrite if the version aliases exists in a existing model.

fields.name: walsAlpha
fields.description: Hyperparameter for matrix factoration when implicit feedback type is specified.

fields.name: warmStart
fields.description: Whether to train a model from the last checkpoint.

fields.name: xgboostVersion
fields.description: User-selected XGBoost versions for training of XGBoost models.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_mlStatistics_hparamTrials_items_hparams_dataFrequency
types.description: The data frequency of a time series.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_mlStatistics_hparamTrials_items_hparams_dataSplitMethod
types.description: The data split type for training and evaluation, e.g. RANDOM.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_mlStatistics_hparamTrials_items_hparams_distanceType
types.description: Distance type for clustering models.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_mlStatistics_hparamTrials_items_hparams_feedbackType
types.description: Feedback type that specifies which algorithm to run for matrix factorization.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_mlStatistics_hparamTrials_items_hparams_holidayRegion
types.description: The geographical region based on which the holidays are considered in time series modeling. If a valid value is specified, then holiday effects modeling is enabled.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_mlStatistics_hparamTrials_items_hparams_kmeansInitializationMethod
types.description: The method used to initialize the centroids for kmeans algorithm.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_mlStatistics_hparamTrials_items_hparams_learnRateStrategy
types.description: The strategy to determine learn rate for the current iteration.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_mlStatistics_hparamTrials_items_hparams_lossType
types.description: Type of loss function used during training run.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_mlStatistics_hparamTrials_items_hparams_modelRegistry
types.description: The model registry.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_mlStatistics_hparamTrials_items_hparams_optimizationStrategy
types.description: Optimization strategy for training linear regression models.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_mlStatistics_hparamTrials_items_hparams_pcaSolver
types.description: The solver for PCA.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_mlStatistics_hparamTrials_items_hparams_reservationAffinityType
types.description: Specifies the reservation affinity type used to configure a Vertex AI resource. The default value is `NO_RESERVATION`.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_mlStatistics_hparamTrials_items_hparams_treeMethod
types.description: Tree construction algorithm for boosted tree models.

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_query_mlStatistics_hparamTrials_items_status
types.description: The status of the trial.

types.name: IterationResult
types.description: Information about a single iteration of the training run.

fields.name: clusterInfos
fields.description: Information about top clusters for clustering models.

fields.name: durationMs
fields.description: Time taken to run the iteration in milliseconds.

fields.name: evalLoss
fields.description: Loss computed on the eval data at the end of iteration.

fields.name: index
fields.description: Index of the iteration, 0 based.

fields.name: learnRate
fields.description: Learn rate used for this iteration.

fields.name: principalComponentInfos
fields.description: The information of the principal components.

fields.name: trainingLoss

fields.name: gcsStagingBucket
fields.description: Output only. The Google Cloud Storage bucket that is used as the default file system by the Spark application. This field is only filled when the Spark procedure uses the invoker security mode. The `gcsStagingBucket` bucket is inferred from the `@@spark_proc_properties.staging_bucket` system variable (if it is provided). Otherwise, BigQuery creates a default staging bucket for the job and returns the bucket name in this field. Example: * `gs://[bucket_name]`

fields.name: kmsKeyName
fields.description: Output only. The Cloud KMS encryption key that is used to protect the resources created by the Spark job. If the Spark procedure uses the invoker security mode, the Cloud KMS encryption key is either inferred from the provided system variable, `@@spark_proc_properties.kms_key_name`, or the default key of the BigQuery job's project (if the CMEK organization policy is enforced). Otherwise, the Cloud KMS key is either inferred from the Spark connection associated with the procedure (if it is provided), or from the default key of the Spark connection's project if the CMEK organization policy is enforced. Example: * `projects/[kms_project_id]/locations/[region]/keyRings/[key_region]/cryptoKeys/[key]`

fields.description: Output only. Spark job ID if a Spark job is created successfully.

fields.name: sparkJobLocation

types.name: mutation_bigquery_jobs_cancel_job_statistics_scriptStatistics_evaluationKind
types.description: Whether this child job was a statement or expression.

types.name: ScriptStackFrame
types.description: Represents the location of the statement/expression being evaluated. Line and column numbers are defined as follows: - Line and column numbers start with one. That is, line 1 column 1 denotes the start of the script. - When inside a stored procedure, all line/column numbers are relative to the procedure body, not the script in which the procedure was defined. - Start/end positions exclude leading/trailing comments and whitespace. The end position always ends with a ";", when present. - Multi-byte Unicode characters are treated as just one column. - If the original script (or procedure definition) contains TAB characters, a tab "snaps" the indentation forward to the nearest multiple of 8 characters, plus 1. For example, a TAB on column 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 , or 8 will advance the next character to column 9. A TAB on column 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, or 16 will advance the next character to column 17.

fields.name: endColumn
fields.description: Output only. One-based end column.

fields.name: endLine
fields.description: Output only. One-based end line.

fields.name: errors

Errors here do not necessarily mean that the job has completed or was unsuccessful. For more information about error messages, see [Error messages](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/error-messages).

fields.description: An object with as many results as can be contained within the maximum permitted reply size. To get any additional rows, you can call GetQueryResults and specify the jobReference returned above. Present only when the query completes successfully. The REST-based representation of this data leverages a series of JSON f,v objects for indicating fields and values.

fields.description: [Full-projection-only] String representation of identity of requesting party. Populated for both first- and third-party identities. Only present for APIs that support third-party identities.

fields.name: defaultTrialId
fields.description: Output only. The default trial_id to use in TVFs when the trial_id is not passed in. For single-objective [hyperparameter tuning](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery-ml/docs/reference/standard-sql/bigqueryml-syntax-hp-tuning-overview) models, this is the best trial ID. For multi-objective [hyperparameter tuning](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery-ml/docs/reference/standard-sql/bigqueryml-syntax-hp-tuning-overview) models, this is the smallest trial ID among all Pareto optimal trials.

fields.description: Optional. A user-friendly description of this model.

fields.name: etag
fields.description: Output only. A hash of this resource.

fields.name: expirationTime
fields.description: Optional. The time when this model expires, in milliseconds since the epoch. If not present, the model will persist indefinitely. Expired models will be deleted and their storage reclaimed. The defaultTableExpirationMs property of the encapsulating dataset can be used to set a default expirationTime on newly created models.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: The labels associated with this model. You can use these to organize and group your models. Label keys and values can be no longer than 63 characters, can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. Label values are optional. Label keys must start with a letter and each label in the list must have a different key.

fields.name: lastModifiedTime

fields.description: Class label for this set of global explanations. Will be empty/null for binary logistic and linear regression models. Sorted alphabetically in descending order.

fields.name: explanations
fields.description: A list of the top global explanations. Sorted by absolute value of attribution in descending order.

types.name: Explanation

fields.name: attribution
fields.description: Attribution of feature.

fields.name: featureName
fields.description: The full feature name. For non-numerical features, will be formatted like `.`. Overall size of feature name will always be truncated to first 120 characters.

types.name: DataSplitResult

types.name: TransformColumn

fields.name: name

fields.name: transformSql
