inputFields.name: timeline
inputFields.description: Output only. Describes a timeline of job execution.

inputFields.name: totalBytesBilled
inputFields.description: Output only. If the project is configured to use on-demand pricing, then this field contains the total bytes billed for the job. If the project is configured to use flat-rate pricing, then you are not billed for bytes and this field is informational only.

inputFields.name: totalBytesProcessed
inputFields.description: Output only. Total bytes processed for the job.

inputFields.name: totalBytesProcessedAccuracy
inputFields.description: Output only. For dry-run jobs, totalBytesProcessed is an estimate and this field specifies the accuracy of the estimate. Possible values can be: UNKNOWN: accuracy of the estimate is unknown. PRECISE: estimate is precise. LOWER_BOUND: estimate is lower bound of what the query would cost. UPPER_BOUND: estimate is upper bound of what the query would cost.

inputFields.name: totalPartitionsProcessed
inputFields.description: Output only. Total number of partitions processed from all partitioned tables referenced in the job.

inputFields.name: totalServicesSkuSlotMs
inputFields.description: Output only. Total slot milliseconds for the job that ran on external services and billed on the services SKU. This field is only populated for jobs that have external service costs, and is the total of the usage for costs whose billing method is `"SERVICES_SKU"`.

inputFields.name: totalSlotMs
inputFields.description: Output only. Slot-milliseconds for the job.

inputFields.name: transferredBytes
inputFields.description: Output only. Total bytes transferred for cross-cloud queries such as Cross Cloud Transfer and CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS).

inputFields.name: undeclaredQueryParameters
inputFields.description: Output only. GoogleSQL only: list of undeclared query parameters detected during a dry run validation.

types.name: BiEngineStatistics_Input
types.description: Statistics for a BI Engine specific query. Populated as part of JobStatistics2

inputFields.name: biEngineReasons
inputFields.description: In case of DISABLED or PARTIAL bi_engine_mode, these contain the explanatory reasons as to why BI Engine could not accelerate. In case the full query was accelerated, this field is not populated.

types.name: BiEngineReason_Input
types.description: Reason why BI Engine didn't accelerate the query (or sub-query).

inputFields.name: message
inputFields.description: Output only. Free form human-readable reason for partial or disabled acceleration.

types.name: RowAccessPolicyReference_Input
types.description: Id path of a row access policy.

inputFields.name: datasetId
inputFields.description: Required. The ID of the dataset containing this row access policy.

inputFields.name: policyId
inputFields.description: Required. The ID of the row access policy. The ID must contain only letters (a-z, A-Z), numbers (0-9), or underscores (_). The maximum length is 256 characters.

inputFields.name: projectId
inputFields.description: Required. The ID of the project containing this row access policy.

inputFields.name: tableId
inputFields.description: Required. The ID of the table containing this row access policy.

types.name: DmlStatistics_Input
types.description: Detailed statistics for DML statements

inputFields.name: deletedRowCount
inputFields.description: Output only. Number of deleted Rows. populated by DML DELETE, MERGE and TRUNCATE statements.

inputFields.name: insertedRowCount
inputFields.description: Output only. Number of inserted Rows. Populated by DML INSERT and MERGE statements

inputFields.name: updatedRowCount
inputFields.description: Output only. Number of updated Rows. Populated by DML UPDATE and MERGE statements.

types.name: ExportDataStatistics_Input
types.description: Statistics for the EXPORT DATA statement as part of Query Job. EXTRACT JOB statistics are populated in JobStatistics4.

inputFields.name: fileCount
inputFields.description: Number of destination files generated in case of EXPORT DATA statement only.

inputFields.name: rowCount
inputFields.description: [Alpha] Number of destination rows generated in case of EXPORT DATA statement only.

types.name: ExternalServiceCost_Input
types.description: The external service cost is a portion of the total cost, these costs are not additive with total_bytes_billed. Moreover, this field only track external service costs that will show up as BigQuery costs (e.g. training BigQuery ML job with google cloud CAIP or Automl Tables services), not other costs which may be accrued by running the query (e.g. reading from Bigtable or Cloud Storage). The external service costs with different billing sku (e.g. CAIP job is charged based on VM usage) are converted to BigQuery billed_bytes and slot_ms with equivalent amount of US dollars. Services may not directly correlate to these metrics, but these are the equivalents for billing purposes. Output only.

inputFields.name: billingMethod
inputFields.description: The billing method used for the external job. This field, set to `SERVICES_SKU`, is only used when billing under the services SKU. Otherwise, it is unspecified for backward compatibility.

inputFields.name: bytesBilled
inputFields.description: External service cost in terms of bigquery bytes billed.

inputFields.name: bytesProcessed
inputFields.description: External service cost in terms of bigquery bytes processed.

inputFields.name: externalService
inputFields.description: External service name.

inputFields.name: reservedSlotCount
inputFields.description: Non-preemptable reserved slots used for external job. For example, reserved slots for Cloua AI Platform job are the VM usages converted to BigQuery slot with equivalent mount of price.

inputFields.name: slotMs
inputFields.description: External service cost in terms of bigquery slot milliseconds.

types.name: GenAiStats_Input
types.description: GenAi stats for the query job.

inputFields.name: functionStats
inputFields.description: Function level stats for GenAi Functions. See https://docs.cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/generative-ai-overview

types.name: GenAiErrorStats_Input
types.description: Provides error statistics for the query job across all AI function calls.

inputFields.name: errors
inputFields.description: A list of unique errors at query level (up to 5, truncated to 100 chars)

types.name: GenAiFunctionStats_Input
types.description: Provides statistics for each Ai function call within a query.

inputFields.name: functionName
inputFields.description: Name of the function.

inputFields.name: numProcessedRows
inputFields.description: Number of rows processed by this GenAi function. This includes all cost_optimized, llm_inferred and failed_rows.

inputFields.name: prompt
inputFields.description: User input prompt of the function (truncated to 20 chars).

types.name: GenAiFunctionCostOptimizationStats_Input
types.description: Provides cost optimization statistics for a GenAi function call.

inputFields.name: message
inputFields.description: System generated message to provide insights into cost optimization state.

inputFields.name: numCostOptimizedRows
inputFields.description: Number of rows inferred via cost optimized workflow.

types.name: GenAiFunctionErrorStats_Input
types.description: Provides error statistics for a GenAi function call.

inputFields.name: errors
inputFields.description: A list of unique errors at function level (up to 5, truncated to 100 chars).

inputFields.name: numFailedRows
inputFields.description: Number of failed rows processed by the function

types.name: IncrementalResultStats_Input
types.description: Statistics related to Incremental Query Results. Populated as part of JobStatistics2. This feature is not yet available.

inputFields.name: disabledReasonDetails
inputFields.description: Output only. Additional human-readable clarification, if available, for DisabledReason.

inputFields.name: firstIncrementalRowTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The time at which the first incremental result was written. If the query needed to restart internally, this only describes the final attempt.

inputFields.name: incrementalRowCount
inputFields.description: Output only. Number of rows that were in the latest result set before query completion.

inputFields.name: lastIncrementalRowTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The time at which the last incremental result was written. Does not include the final result written after query completion.

inputFields.name: resultSetLastModifyTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The time at which the result table's contents were modified. May be absent if no results have been written or the query has completed.

inputFields.name: resultSetLastReplaceTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The time at which the result table's contents were completely replaced. May be absent if no results have been written or the query has completed.

types.name: LoadQueryStatistics_Input
types.description: Statistics for a LOAD query.

inputFields.name: badRecords
inputFields.description: Output only. The number of bad records encountered while processing a LOAD query. Note that if the job has failed because of more bad records encountered than the maximum allowed in the load job configuration, then this number can be less than the total number of bad records present in the input data.

inputFields.name: bytesTransferred
inputFields.description: Output only. This field is deprecated. The number of bytes of source data copied over the network for a `LOAD` query. `transferred_bytes` has the canonical value for physical transferred bytes, which is used for BigQuery Omni billing.

inputFields.name: inputFileBytes
inputFields.description: Output only. Number of bytes of source data in a LOAD query.

inputFields.name: inputFiles
inputFields.description: Output only. Number of source files in a LOAD query.

inputFields.name: outputBytes
inputFields.description: Output only. Size of the loaded data in bytes. Note that while a LOAD query is in the running state, this value may change.

inputFields.name: outputRows
inputFields.description: Output only. Number of rows imported in a LOAD query. Note that while a LOAD query is in the running state, this value may change.

types.name: MaterializedViewStatistics_Input
types.description: Statistics of materialized views considered in a query job.

inputFields.name: materializedView
inputFields.description: Materialized views considered for the query job. Only certain materialized views are used. For a detailed list, see the child message. If many materialized views are considered, then the list might be incomplete.

types.name: MaterializedView_Input
types.description: A materialized view considered for a query job.

inputFields.name: chosen
inputFields.description: Whether the materialized view is chosen for the query. A materialized view can be chosen to rewrite multiple parts of the same query. If a materialized view is chosen to rewrite any part of the query, then this field is true, even if the materialized view was not chosen to rewrite others parts.

inputFields.name: estimatedBytesSaved
inputFields.description: If present, specifies a best-effort estimation of the bytes saved by using the materialized view rather than its base tables.

types.name: MetadataCacheStatistics_Input
types.description: Statistics for metadata caching in queried tables.

inputFields.name: tableMetadataCacheUsage
inputFields.description: Set for the Metadata caching eligible tables referenced in the query.

types.name: TableMetadataCacheUsage_Input
types.description: Table level detail on the usage of metadata caching. Only set for Metadata caching eligible tables referenced in the query.

inputFields.name: explanation
inputFields.description: Free form human-readable reason metadata caching was unused for the job.

inputFields.name: staleness
inputFields.description: Duration since last refresh as of this job for managed tables (indicates metadata cache staleness as seen by this job).

inputFields.name: tableType
inputFields.description: [Table type](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/rest/v2/tables#Table.FIELDS.type).

types.name: PruningStats_Input
types.description: The column metadata index pruning statistics.

inputFields.name: postCmetaPruningParallelInputCount
inputFields.description: The number of parallel inputs matched.

inputFields.name: postCmetaPruningPartitionCount
inputFields.description: The number of partitions matched.

inputFields.name: preCmetaPruningParallelInputCount
inputFields.description: The number of parallel inputs scanned.

types.name: MlStatistics_Input
types.description: Job statistics specific to a BigQuery ML training job.

inputFields.name: hparamTrials
inputFields.description: Output only. Trials of a [hyperparameter tuning job](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery-ml/docs/reference/standard-sql/bigqueryml-syntax-hp-tuning-overview) sorted by trial_id.

inputFields.name: iterationResults
inputFields.description: Results for all completed iterations. Empty for [hyperparameter tuning jobs](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery-ml/docs/reference/standard-sql/bigqueryml-syntax-hp-tuning-overview).

inputFields.name: maxIterations
inputFields.description: Output only. Maximum number of iterations specified as max_iterations in the 'CREATE MODEL' query. The actual number of iterations may be less than this number due to early stop.

types.name: HparamTuningTrial_Input
types.description: Training info of a trial in [hyperparameter tuning](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery-ml/docs/reference/standard-sql/bigqueryml-syntax-hp-tuning-overview) models.

inputFields.name: endTimeMs
inputFields.description: Ending time of the trial.

inputFields.name: errorMessage
inputFields.description: Error message for FAILED and INFEASIBLE trial.

inputFields.name: evalLoss
inputFields.description: Loss computed on the eval data at the end of trial.

inputFields.name: startTimeMs
inputFields.description: Starting time of the trial.

inputFields.name: trainingLoss
inputFields.description: Loss computed on the training data at the end of trial.

inputFields.name: trialId
inputFields.description: 1-based index of the trial.

types.name: EvaluationMetrics_Input
types.description: Evaluation metrics of a model. These are either computed on all training data or just the eval data based on whether eval data was used during training. These are not present for imported models.

types.name: ArimaForecastingMetrics_Input
types.description: Model evaluation metrics for ARIMA forecasting models.

inputFields.name: arimaFittingMetrics
inputFields.description: Arima model fitting metrics.

inputFields.name: arimaSingleModelForecastingMetrics
inputFields.description: Repeated as there can be many metric sets (one for each model) in auto-arima and the large-scale case.

inputFields.name: hasDrift
inputFields.description: Whether Arima model fitted with drift or not. It is always false when d is not 1.

inputFields.name: nonSeasonalOrder
inputFields.description: Non-seasonal order.

inputFields.name: seasonalPeriods
inputFields.description: Seasonal periods. Repeated because multiple periods are supported for one time series.

inputFields.name: timeSeriesId
inputFields.description: Id to differentiate different time series for the large-scale case.

types.name: ArimaFittingMetrics_Input
types.description: ARIMA model fitting metrics.

inputFields.name: aic
inputFields.description: AIC.

inputFields.name: logLikelihood
inputFields.description: Log-likelihood.

inputFields.name: variance
inputFields.description: Variance.

types.name: ArimaSingleModelForecastingMetrics_Input
types.description: Model evaluation metrics for a single ARIMA forecasting model.

inputFields.name: hasDrift
inputFields.description: Is arima model fitted with drift or not. It is always false when d is not 1.

inputFields.name: hasHolidayEffect
inputFields.description: If true, holiday_effect is a part of time series decomposition result.

inputFields.name: hasSpikesAndDips
inputFields.description: If true, spikes_and_dips is a part of time series decomposition result.

inputFields.name: hasStepChanges
inputFields.description: If true, step_changes is a part of time series decomposition result.

inputFields.name: seasonalPeriods
inputFields.description: Seasonal periods. Repeated because multiple periods are supported for one time series.

inputFields.name: timeSeriesId
inputFields.description: The time_series_id value for this time series. It will be one of the unique values from the time_series_id_column specified during ARIMA model training. Only present when time_series_id_column training option was used.

inputFields.name: timeSeriesIds
inputFields.description: The tuple of time_series_ids identifying this time series. It will be one of the unique tuples of values present in the time_series_id_columns specified during ARIMA model training. Only present when time_series_id_columns training option was used and the order of values here are same as the order of time_series_id_columns.

types.name: ArimaOrder_Input
types.description: Arima order, can be used for both non-seasonal and seasonal parts.

inputFields.name: d
inputFields.description: Order of the differencing part.

inputFields.name: p
inputFields.description: Order of the autoregressive part.

inputFields.name: q
inputFields.description: Order of the moving-average part.

types.name: BinaryClassificationMetrics_Input
types.description: Evaluation metrics for binary classification/classifier models.

inputFields.name: binaryConfusionMatrixList
inputFields.description: Binary confusion matrix at multiple thresholds.

inputFields.name: negativeLabel
inputFields.description: Label representing the negative class.

inputFields.name: positiveLabel
inputFields.description: Label representing the positive class.

types.name: AggregateClassificationMetrics_Input
types.description: Aggregate metrics for classification/classifier models. For multi-class models, the metrics are either macro-averaged or micro-averaged. When macro-averaged, the metrics are calculated for each label and then an unweighted average is taken of those values. When micro-averaged, the metric is calculated globally by counting the total number of correctly predicted rows.

inputFields.name: accuracy
inputFields.description: Accuracy is the fraction of predictions given the correct label. For multiclass this is a micro-averaged metric.

inputFields.name: f1Score
inputFields.description: The F1 score is an average of recall and precision. For multiclass this is a macro-averaged metric.

inputFields.name: logLoss
inputFields.description: Logarithmic Loss. For multiclass this is a macro-averaged metric.

inputFields.name: precision
inputFields.description: Precision is the fraction of actual positive predictions that had positive actual labels. For multiclass this is a macro-averaged metric treating each class as a binary classifier.

inputFields.name: recall
inputFields.description: Recall is the fraction of actual positive labels that were given a positive prediction. For multiclass this is a macro-averaged metric.

inputFields.name: rocAuc
inputFields.description: Area Under a ROC Curve. For multiclass this is a macro-averaged metric.

inputFields.name: threshold
inputFields.description: Threshold at which the metrics are computed. For binary classification models this is the positive class threshold. For multi-class classification models this is the confidence threshold.

types.name: BinaryConfusionMatrix_Input
types.description: Confusion matrix for binary classification models.

inputFields.name: accuracy
inputFields.description: The fraction of predictions given the correct label.

inputFields.name: f1Score
inputFields.description: The equally weighted average of recall and precision.

inputFields.name: falseNegatives
inputFields.description: Number of false samples predicted as false.

inputFields.name: falsePositives
inputFields.description: Number of false samples predicted as true.

inputFields.name: positiveClassThreshold
inputFields.description: Threshold value used when computing each of the following metric.

inputFields.name: precision
inputFields.description: The fraction of actual positive predictions that had positive actual labels.

inputFields.name: recall
inputFields.description: The fraction of actual positive labels that were given a positive prediction.

inputFields.name: trueNegatives
inputFields.description: Number of true samples predicted as false.

inputFields.name: truePositives
inputFields.description: Number of true samples predicted as true.

types.name: ClusteringMetrics_Input
types.description: Evaluation metrics for clustering models.

inputFields.name: clusters
inputFields.description: Information for all clusters.

inputFields.name: daviesBouldinIndex
inputFields.description: Davies-Bouldin index.

inputFields.name: meanSquaredDistance
inputFields.description: Mean of squared distances between each sample to its cluster centroid.

types.name: Cluster_Input
types.description: Message containing the information about one cluster.

inputFields.name: centroidId
inputFields.description: Centroid id.

inputFields.name: count
inputFields.description: Count of training data rows that were assigned to this cluster.

inputFields.name: featureValues
inputFields.description: Values of highly variant features for this cluster.

types.name: FeatureValue_Input
types.description: Representative value of a single feature within the cluster.

inputFields.name: featureColumn
inputFields.description: The feature column name.

inputFields.name: numericalValue
inputFields.description: The numerical feature value. This is the centroid value for this feature.

types.name: CategoricalValue_Input
types.description: Representative value of a categorical feature.

inputFields.name: categoryCounts
inputFields.description: Counts of all categories for the categorical feature. If there are more than ten categories, we return top ten (by count) and return one more CategoryCount with category "_OTHER_" and count as aggregate counts of remaining categories.

types.name: CategoryCount_Input
types.description: Represents the count of a single category within the cluster.

inputFields.name: category
inputFields.description: The name of category.

inputFields.name: count
inputFields.description: The count of training samples matching the category within the cluster.

types.name: DimensionalityReductionMetrics_Input
types.description: Model evaluation metrics for dimensionality reduction models.

inputFields.name: totalExplainedVarianceRatio
inputFields.description: Total percentage of variance explained by the selected principal components.

types.name: MultiClassClassificationMetrics_Input
types.description: Evaluation metrics for multi-class classification/classifier models.

inputFields.name: confusionMatrixList
inputFields.description: Confusion matrix at different thresholds.

types.name: ConfusionMatrix_Input
types.description: Confusion matrix for multi-class classification models.

inputFields.name: confidenceThreshold
inputFields.description: Confidence threshold used when computing the entries of the confusion matrix.

inputFields.name: rows
inputFields.description: One row per actual label.

types.name: Row_Input
types.description: A single row in the confusion matrix.

inputFields.name: actualLabel
inputFields.description: The original label of this row.

inputFields.name: entries
inputFields.description: Info describing predicted label distribution.

types.name: Entry_Input
types.description: A single entry in the confusion matrix.

inputFields.name: itemCount
inputFields.description: Number of items being predicted as this label.

inputFields.name: predictedLabel
inputFields.description: The predicted label. For confidence_threshold > 0, we will also add an entry indicating the number of items under the confidence threshold.

types.name: RankingMetrics_Input
types.description: Evaluation metrics used by weighted-ALS models specified by feedback_type=implicit.

inputFields.name: averageRank
inputFields.description: Determines the goodness of a ranking by computing the percentile rank from the predicted confidence and dividing it by the original rank.

inputFields.name: meanAveragePrecision
inputFields.description: Calculates a precision per user for all the items by ranking them and then averages all the precisions across all the users.

inputFields.name: meanSquaredError
inputFields.description: Similar to the mean squared error computed in regression and explicit recommendation models except instead of computing the rating directly, the output from evaluate is computed against a preference which is 1 or 0 depending on if the rating exists or not.

inputFields.name: normalizedDiscountedCumulativeGain
inputFields.description: A metric to determine the goodness of a ranking calculated from the predicted confidence by comparing it to an ideal rank measured by the original ratings.

types.name: RegressionMetrics_Input
types.description: Evaluation metrics for regression and explicit feedback type matrix factorization models.

inputFields.name: meanAbsoluteError
inputFields.description: Mean absolute error.

inputFields.name: meanSquaredError
inputFields.description: Mean squared error.

inputFields.name: meanSquaredLogError
inputFields.description: Mean squared log error.

inputFields.name: medianAbsoluteError
inputFields.description: Median absolute error.

inputFields.name: rSquared
inputFields.description: R^2 score. This corresponds to r2_score in ML.EVALUATE.

types.name: TrainingOptions_Input
types.description: Options used in model training.

inputFields.name: activationFn
inputFields.description: Activation function of the neural nets.

inputFields.name: adjustStepChanges
inputFields.description: If true, detect step changes and make data adjustment in the input time series.

inputFields.name: approxGlobalFeatureContrib
inputFields.description: Whether to use approximate feature contribution method in XGBoost model explanation for global explain.

inputFields.name: autoArima
inputFields.description: Whether to enable auto ARIMA or not.

inputFields.name: autoArimaMaxOrder
inputFields.description: The max value of the sum of non-seasonal p and q.

inputFields.name: autoArimaMinOrder
inputFields.description: The min value of the sum of non-seasonal p and q.

inputFields.name: autoClassWeights
inputFields.description: Whether to calculate class weights automatically based on the popularity of each label.

inputFields.name: batchSize
inputFields.description: Batch size for dnn models.

inputFields.name: budgetHours
inputFields.description: Budget in hours for AutoML training.

inputFields.name: calculatePValues
inputFields.description: Whether or not p-value test should be computed for this model. Only available for linear and logistic regression models.

inputFields.name: cleanSpikesAndDips
inputFields.description: If true, clean spikes and dips in the input time series.

inputFields.name: colsampleBylevel
inputFields.description: Subsample ratio of columns for each level for boosted tree models.

inputFields.name: colsampleBynode
inputFields.description: Subsample ratio of columns for each node(split) for boosted tree models.

inputFields.name: colsampleBytree
inputFields.description: Subsample ratio of columns when constructing each tree for boosted tree models.

inputFields.name: contributionMetric
inputFields.description: The contribution metric. Applies to contribution analysis models. Allowed formats supported are for summable and summable ratio contribution metrics. These include expressions such as `SUM(x)` or `SUM(x)/SUM(y)`, where x and y are column names from the base table.

inputFields.name: dataSplitColumn
inputFields.description: The column to split data with. This column won't be used as a feature. 1. When data_split_method is CUSTOM, the corresponding column should be boolean. The rows with true value tag are eval data, and the false are training data. 2. When data_split_method is SEQ, the first DATA_SPLIT_EVAL_FRACTION rows (from smallest to largest) in the corresponding column are used as training data, and the rest are eval data. It respects the order in Orderable data types: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/standard-sql/data-types#data_type_properties

inputFields.name: dataSplitEvalFraction
inputFields.description: The fraction of evaluation data over the whole input data. The rest of data will be used as training data. The format should be double. Accurate to two decimal places. Default value is 0.2.

inputFields.name: decomposeTimeSeries
inputFields.description: If true, perform decompose time series and save the results.

inputFields.name: dimensionIdColumns
inputFields.description: Optional. Names of the columns to slice on. Applies to contribution analysis models.

inputFields.name: dropout
inputFields.description: Dropout probability for dnn models.

inputFields.name: earlyStop
inputFields.description: Whether to stop early when the loss doesn't improve significantly any more (compared to min_relative_progress). Used only for iterative training algorithms.

inputFields.name: enableGlobalExplain
inputFields.description: If true, enable global explanation during training.

inputFields.name: endpointIdleTtl
inputFields.description: The idle TTL of the endpoint before the resources get destroyed. The default value is 6.5 hours.

inputFields.name: fitIntercept
inputFields.description: Whether the model should include intercept during model training.

inputFields.name: forecastLimitLowerBound
inputFields.description: The forecast limit lower bound that was used during ARIMA model training with limits. To see more details of the algorithm: https://otexts.com/fpp2/limits.html

inputFields.name: forecastLimitUpperBound
inputFields.description: The forecast limit upper bound that was used during ARIMA model training with limits.

inputFields.name: hiddenUnits
inputFields.description: Hidden units for dnn models.

inputFields.name: holidayRegions
inputFields.description: A list of geographical regions that are used for time series modeling.

inputFields.name: horizon
inputFields.description: The number of periods ahead that need to be forecasted.

inputFields.name: hparamTuningObjectives
inputFields.description: The target evaluation metrics to optimize the hyperparameters for.

inputFields.name: huggingFaceModelId
inputFields.description: The id of a Hugging Face model. For example, `google/gemma-2-2b-it`.

inputFields.name: includeDrift
inputFields.description: Include drift when fitting an ARIMA model.

inputFields.name: initialLearnRate
inputFields.description: Specifies the initial learning rate for the line search learn rate strategy.

inputFields.name: inputLabelColumns
inputFields.description: Name of input label columns in training data.

inputFields.name: instanceWeightColumn
inputFields.description: Name of the instance weight column for training data. This column isn't be used as a feature.

inputFields.name: integratedGradientsNumSteps
inputFields.description: Number of integral steps for the integrated gradients explain method.

inputFields.name: isTestColumn
inputFields.description: Name of the column used to determine the rows corresponding to control and test. Applies to contribution analysis models.

inputFields.name: itemColumn
inputFields.description: Item column specified for matrix factorization models.

inputFields.name: kmeansInitializationColumn
inputFields.description: The column used to provide the initial centroids for kmeans algorithm when kmeans_initialization_method is CUSTOM.

inputFields.name: l1RegActivation
inputFields.description: L1 regularization coefficient to activations.

inputFields.name: l1Regularization
inputFields.description: L1 regularization coefficient.

inputFields.name: l2Regularization
inputFields.description: L2 regularization coefficient.

inputFields.name: labelClassWeights
inputFields.description: Weights associated with each label class, for rebalancing the training data. Only applicable for classification models.

inputFields.name: learnRate
inputFields.description: Learning rate in training. Used only for iterative training algorithms.

inputFields.name: machineType
inputFields.description: The type of the machine used to deploy and serve the model.

inputFields.name: maxIterations
inputFields.description: The maximum number of iterations in training. Used only for iterative training algorithms.

inputFields.name: maxParallelTrials
inputFields.description: Maximum number of trials to run in parallel.

inputFields.name: maxReplicaCount
inputFields.description: The maximum number of machine replicas that will be deployed on an endpoint. The default value is equal to min_replica_count.

inputFields.name: maxTimeSeriesLength
inputFields.description: The maximum number of time points in a time series that can be used in modeling the trend component of the time series. Don't use this option with the `timeSeriesLengthFraction` or `minTimeSeriesLength` options.

inputFields.name: maxTreeDepth
inputFields.description: Maximum depth of a tree for boosted tree models.

inputFields.name: minAprioriSupport
inputFields.description: The apriori support minimum. Applies to contribution analysis models.

inputFields.name: minRelativeProgress
inputFields.description: When early_stop is true, stops training when accuracy improvement is less than 'min_relative_progress'. Used only for iterative training algorithms.

inputFields.name: minReplicaCount
inputFields.description: The minimum number of machine replicas that will be always deployed on an endpoint. This value must be greater than or equal to 1. The default value is 1.

inputFields.name: minSplitLoss
inputFields.description: Minimum split loss for boosted tree models.

inputFields.name: minTimeSeriesLength
inputFields.description: The minimum number of time points in a time series that are used in modeling the trend component of the time series. If you use this option you must also set the `timeSeriesLengthFraction` option. This training option ensures that enough time points are available when you use `timeSeriesLengthFraction` in trend modeling. This is particularly important when forecasting multiple time series in a single query using `timeSeriesIdColumn`. If the total number of time points is less than the `minTimeSeriesLength` value, then the query uses all available time points.

inputFields.name: minTreeChildWeight
inputFields.description: Minimum sum of instance weight needed in a child for boosted tree models.

inputFields.name: modelGardenModelName
inputFields.description: The name of a Vertex model garden publisher model. Format is `publishers/{publisher}/models/{model}@{optional_version_id}`.

inputFields.name: modelUri
inputFields.description: Google Cloud Storage URI from which the model was imported. Only applicable for imported models.

inputFields.name: numClusters
inputFields.description: Number of clusters for clustering models.

inputFields.name: numFactors
inputFields.description: Num factors specified for matrix factorization models.

inputFields.name: numParallelTree
inputFields.description: Number of parallel trees constructed during each iteration for boosted tree models.

inputFields.name: numPrincipalComponents
inputFields.description: Number of principal components to keep in the PCA model. Must be <= the number of features.

inputFields.name: numTrials
inputFields.description: Number of trials to run this hyperparameter tuning job.

inputFields.name: optimizer
inputFields.description: Optimizer used for training the neural nets.

inputFields.name: pcaExplainedVarianceRatio
inputFields.description: The minimum ratio of cumulative explained variance that needs to be given by the PCA model.

inputFields.name: reservationAffinityKey
inputFields.description: Corresponds to the label key of a reservation resource used by Vertex AI. To target a SPECIFIC_RESERVATION by name, use `compute.googleapis.com/reservation-name` as the key and specify the name of your reservation as its value.

inputFields.name: reservationAffinityValues
inputFields.description: Corresponds to the label values of a reservation resource used by Vertex AI. This must be the full resource name of the reservation or reservation block.

inputFields.name: sampledShapleyNumPaths
inputFields.description: Number of paths for the sampled Shapley explain method.

inputFields.name: scaleFeatures
inputFields.description: If true, scale the feature values by dividing the feature standard deviation. Currently only apply to PCA.

inputFields.name: standardizeFeatures
inputFields.description: Whether to standardize numerical features. Default to true.

inputFields.name: subsample
inputFields.description: Subsample fraction of the training data to grow tree to prevent overfitting for boosted tree models.

inputFields.name: tfVersion
inputFields.description: Based on the selected TF version, the corresponding docker image is used to train external models.

inputFields.name: timeSeriesDataColumn
inputFields.description: Column to be designated as time series data for ARIMA model.

inputFields.name: timeSeriesIdColumn
inputFields.description: The time series id column that was used during ARIMA model training.

inputFields.name: timeSeriesIdColumns
inputFields.description: The time series id columns that were used during ARIMA model training.

inputFields.name: timeSeriesLengthFraction
inputFields.description: The fraction of the interpolated length of the time series that's used to model the time series trend component. All of the time points of the time series are used to model the non-trend component. This training option accelerates modeling training without sacrificing much forecasting accuracy. You can use this option with `minTimeSeriesLength` but not with `maxTimeSeriesLength`.

inputFields.name: timeSeriesTimestampColumn
inputFields.description: Column to be designated as time series timestamp for ARIMA model.

inputFields.name: trendSmoothingWindowSize
inputFields.description: Smoothing window size for the trend component. When a positive value is specified, a center moving average smoothing is applied on the history trend. When the smoothing window is out of the boundary at the beginning or the end of the trend, the first element or the last element is padded to fill the smoothing window before the average is applied.

inputFields.name: userColumn
inputFields.description: User column specified for matrix factorization models.

inputFields.name: vertexAiModelVersionAliases
inputFields.description: The version aliases to apply in Vertex AI model registry. Always overwrite if the version aliases exists in a existing model.

inputFields.name: walsAlpha
inputFields.description: Hyperparameter for matrix factoration when implicit feedback type is specified.

inputFields.name: warmStart
inputFields.description: Whether to train a model from the last checkpoint.

inputFields.name: xgboostVersion
inputFields.description: User-selected XGBoost versions for training of XGBoost models.

types.name: IterationResult_Input
types.description: Information about a single iteration of the training run.

inputFields.name: clusterInfos
inputFields.description: Information about top clusters for clustering models.

inputFields.name: durationMs
inputFields.description: Time taken to run the iteration in milliseconds.

inputFields.name: evalLoss
inputFields.description: Loss computed on the eval data at the end of iteration.

inputFields.name: index
inputFields.description: Index of the iteration, 0 based.

inputFields.name: learnRate
inputFields.description: Learn rate used for this iteration.

inputFields.name: principalComponentInfos
inputFields.description: The information of the principal components.

inputFields.name: trainingLoss
inputFields.description: Loss computed on the training data at the end of iteration.

types.name: ArimaResult_Input
types.description: (Auto-)arima fitting result. Wrap everything in ArimaResult for easier refactoring if we want to use model-specific iteration results.

inputFields.name: arimaModelInfo
inputFields.description: This message is repeated because there are multiple arima models fitted in auto-arima. For non-auto-arima model, its size is one.

inputFields.name: seasonalPeriods
inputFields.description: Seasonal periods. Repeated because multiple periods are supported for one time series.

types.name: ArimaModelInfo_Input
types.description: Arima model information.

inputFields.name: hasDrift
inputFields.description: Whether Arima model fitted with drift or not. It is always false when d is not 1.

inputFields.name: hasHolidayEffect
inputFields.description: If true, holiday_effect is a part of time series decomposition result.

inputFields.name: hasSpikesAndDips
inputFields.description: If true, spikes_and_dips is a part of time series decomposition result.

inputFields.name: hasStepChanges
inputFields.description: If true, step_changes is a part of time series decomposition result.

inputFields.name: seasonalPeriods
inputFields.description: Seasonal periods. Repeated because multiple periods are supported for one time series.

inputFields.name: timeSeriesId
inputFields.description: The time_series_id value for this time series. It will be one of the unique values from the time_series_id_column specified during ARIMA model training. Only present when time_series_id_column training option was used.

inputFields.name: timeSeriesIds
inputFields.description: The tuple of time_series_ids identifying this time series. It will be one of the unique tuples of values present in the time_series_id_columns specified during ARIMA model training. Only present when time_series_id_columns training option was used and the order of values here are same as the order of time_series_id_columns.

types.name: ArimaCoefficients_Input

inputFields.name: autoRegressiveCoefficients
inputFields.description: Auto-regressive coefficients, an array of double.

inputFields.name: interceptCoefficient
inputFields.description: Intercept coefficient, just a double not an array.

inputFields.name: movingAverageCoefficients
inputFields.description: Moving-average coefficients, an array of double.

inputFields.name: centroidId
inputFields.description: Centroid id.

inputFields.name: clusterRadius
inputFields.description: Cluster radius, the average distance from centroid to each point assigned to the cluster.

inputFields.name: clusterSize
inputFields.description: Cluster size, the total number of points assigned to the cluster.

types.name: PrincipalComponentInfo_Input
types.description: Principal component infos, used only for eigen decomposition based models, e.g., PCA. Ordered by explained_variance in the descending order.

inputFields.name: cumulativeExplainedVarianceRatio
inputFields.description: The explained_variance is pre-ordered in the descending order to compute the cumulative explained variance ratio.

inputFields.description: Explained_variance over the total explained variance.

types.name: SparkStatistics_Input
types.description: Statistics for a BigSpark query. Populated as part of JobStatistics2

inputFields.name: endpoints
inputFields.description: Output only. Endpoints returned from Dataproc. Key list: - history_server_endpoint: A link to Spark job UI.

inputFields.name: gcsStagingBucket
inputFields.description: Output only. The Google Cloud Storage bucket that is used as the default file system by the Spark application. This field is only filled when the Spark procedure uses the invoker security mode. The `gcsStagingBucket` bucket is inferred from the `@@spark_proc_properties.staging_bucket` system variable (if it is provided). Otherwise, BigQuery creates a default staging bucket for the job and returns the bucket name in this field. Example: * `gs://[bucket_name]`

inputFields.name: kmsKeyName
inputFields.description: Output only. The Cloud KMS encryption key that is used to protect the resources created by the Spark job. If the Spark procedure uses the invoker security mode, the Cloud KMS encryption key is either inferred from the provided system variable, `@@spark_proc_properties.kms_key_name`, or the default key of the BigQuery job's project (if the CMEK organization policy is enforced). Otherwise, the Cloud KMS key is either inferred from the Spark connection associated with the procedure (if it is provided), or from the default key of the Spark connection's project if the CMEK organization policy is enforced. Example: * `projects/[kms_project_id]/locations/[region]/keyRings/[key_region]/cryptoKeys/[key]`

inputFields.name: sparkJobId
inputFields.description: Output only. Spark job ID if a Spark job is created successfully.

inputFields.name: sparkJobLocation
inputFields.description: Output only. Location where the Spark job is executed. A location is selected by BigQueury for jobs configured to run in a multi-region.

inputFields.description: Stack trace showing the line/column/procedure name of each frame on the stack at the point where the current evaluation happened. The leaf frame is first, the primary script is last. Never empty.

types.name: ScriptStackFrame_Input
types.description: Represents the location of the statement/expression being evaluated. Line and column numbers are defined as follows: - Line and column numbers start with one. That is, line 1 column 1 denotes the start of the script. - When inside a stored procedure, all line/column numbers are relative to the procedure body, not the script in which the procedure was defined. - Start/end positions exclude leading/trailing comments and whitespace. The end position always ends with a ";", when present. - Multi-byte Unicode characters are treated as just one column. - If the original script (or procedure definition) contains TAB characters, a tab "snaps" the indentation forward to the nearest multiple of 8 characters, plus 1. For example, a TAB on column 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 , or 8 will advance the next character to column 9. A TAB on column 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, or 16 will advance the next character to column 17.

inputFields.name: endColumn
inputFields.description: Output only. One-based end column.

inputFields.name: endLine
inputFields.description: Output only. One-based end line.

inputFields.name: procedureId
inputFields.description: Output only. Name of the active procedure, empty if in a top-level script.

inputFields.name: startColumn
inputFields.description: Output only. One-based start column.

inputFields.name: startLine
inputFields.description: Output only. One-based start line.

inputFields.name: text
inputFields.description: Output only. Text of the current statement/expression.

inputFields.name: state

inputFields.description: Debugging information. This property is internal to Google and should not be used.

inputFields.name: location
inputFields.description: Specifies where the error occurred, if present.

inputFields.name: message
inputFields.description: A human-readable description of the error.

inputFields.name: reason
inputFields.description: A short error code that summarizes the error.

fields.name: cacheHit
fields.description: Whether the query result was fetched from the query cache.

fields.name: creationTime
fields.description: Output only. Creation time of this query, in milliseconds since the epoch. This field will be present on all queries.

fields.name: endTime
fields.description: Output only. End time of this query, in milliseconds since the epoch. This field will be present whenever a query job is in the DONE state.

fields.name: errors
fields.description: Output only. The first errors or warnings encountered during the running of the job. The final message includes the number of errors that caused the process to stop. Errors here do not necessarily mean that the job has completed or was unsuccessful. For more information about error messages, see [Error messages](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/error-messages).

fields.name: jobComplete
fields.description: Whether the query has completed or not. If rows or totalRows are present, this will always be true. If this is false, totalRows will not be available.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: The resource type.

fields.name: location
fields.description: Output only. The geographic location of the query. For more information about BigQuery locations, see: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/locations

fields.name: numDmlAffectedRows
fields.description: Output only. The number of rows affected by a DML statement. Present only for DML statements INSERT, UPDATE or DELETE.

fields.name: pageToken
fields.description: A token used for paging results. A non-empty token indicates that additional results are available. To see additional results, query the [`jobs.getQueryResults`](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/rest/v2/jobs/getQueryResults) method. For more information, see [Paging through table data](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/paging-results).

fields.name: queryId
fields.description: Auto-generated ID for the query.

fields.name: rows
fields.description: An object with as many results as can be contained within the maximum permitted reply size. To get any additional rows, you can call GetQueryResults and specify the jobReference returned above.

fields.name: startTime
fields.description: Output only. Start time of this query, in milliseconds since the epoch. This field will be present when the query job transitions from the PENDING state to either RUNNING or DONE.

fields.name: totalBytesBilled
fields.description: Output only. If the project is configured to use on-demand pricing, then this field contains the total bytes billed for the job. If the project is configured to use flat-rate pricing, then you are not billed for bytes and this field is informational only.

fields.name: totalBytesProcessed
fields.description: The total number of bytes processed for this query. If this query was a dry run, this is the number of bytes that would be processed if the query were run.

fields.name: totalRows
fields.description: The total number of rows in the complete query result set, which can be more than the number of rows in this single page of results.

fields.name: totalSlotMs
fields.description: Output only. Number of slot ms the user is actually billed for.

types.name: QueryRequest_Input
types.description: Describes the format of the jobs.query request.

inputFields.name: connectionProperties
inputFields.description: Optional. Connection properties which can modify the query behavior.

inputFields.name: continuous
inputFields.description: [Optional] Specifies whether the query should be executed as a continuous query. The default value is false.

inputFields.name: createSession
inputFields.description: Optional. If true, creates a new session using a randomly generated session_id. If false, runs query with an existing session_id passed in ConnectionProperty, otherwise runs query in non-session mode. The session location will be set to QueryRequest.location if it is present, otherwise it's set to the default location based on existing routing logic.

inputFields.name: dryRun
inputFields.description: Optional. If set to true, BigQuery doesn't run the job. Instead, if the query is valid, BigQuery returns statistics about the job such as how many bytes would be processed. If the query is invalid, an error returns. The default value is false.

inputFields.name: jobTimeoutMs
inputFields.description: Optional. Job timeout in milliseconds. If this time limit is exceeded, BigQuery will attempt to stop a longer job, but may not always succeed in canceling it before the job completes. For example, a job that takes more than 60 seconds to complete has a better chance of being stopped than a job that takes 10 seconds to complete. This timeout applies to the query even if a job does not need to be created.

inputFields.name: kind
inputFields.description: The resource type of the request.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: Optional. The labels associated with this query. Labels can be used to organize and group query jobs. Label keys and values can be no longer than 63 characters, can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. Label keys must start with a letter and each label in the list must have a different key.

inputFields.name: location
inputFields.description: The geographic location where the job should run. For more information, see how to [specify locations](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/locations#specify_locations).

inputFields.name: maxResults
inputFields.description: Optional. The maximum number of rows of data to return per page of results. Setting this flag to a small value such as 1000 and then paging through results might improve reliability when the query result set is large. In addition to this limit, responses are also limited to 10 MB. By default, there is no maximum row count, and only the byte limit applies.

inputFields.name: maxSlots
inputFields.description: Optional. A target limit on the rate of slot consumption by this query. If set to a value > 0, BigQuery will attempt to limit the rate of slot consumption by this query to keep it below the configured limit, even if the query is eligible for more slots based on fair scheduling. The unused slots will be available for other jobs and queries to use. Note: This feature is not yet generally available.

inputFields.name: maximumBytesBilled
inputFields.description: Optional. Limits the bytes billed for this query. Queries with bytes billed above this limit will fail (without incurring a charge). If unspecified, the project default is used.

inputFields.name: parameterMode
inputFields.description: GoogleSQL only. Set to POSITIONAL to use positional (?) query parameters or to NAMED to use named (@myparam) query parameters in this query.

inputFields.name: preserveNulls
inputFields.description: This property is deprecated.

inputFields.name: query
inputFields.description: Required. A query string to execute, using Google Standard SQL or legacy SQL syntax. Example: "SELECT COUNT(f1) FROM myProjectId.myDatasetId.myTableId".

inputFields.name: queryParameters
inputFields.description: Query parameters for GoogleSQL queries.

inputFields.name: requestId
inputFields.description: Optional. A unique user provided identifier to ensure idempotent behavior for queries. Note that this is different from the job_id. It has the following properties: 1. It is case-sensitive, limited to up to 36 ASCII characters. A UUID is recommended. 2. Read only queries can ignore this token since they are nullipotent by definition. 3. For the purposes of idempotency ensured by the request_id, a request is considered duplicate of another only if they have the same request_id and are actually duplicates. When determining whether a request is a duplicate of another request, all parameters in the request that may affect the result are considered. For example, query, connection_properties, query_parameters, use_legacy_sql are parameters that affect the result and are considered when determining whether a request is a duplicate, but properties like timeout_ms don't affect the result and are thus not considered. Dry run query requests are never considered duplicate of another request. 4. When a duplicate mutating query request is detected, it returns: a. the results of the mutation if it completes successfully within the timeout. b. the running operation if it is still in progress at the end of the timeout. 5. Its lifetime is limited to 15 minutes. In other words, if two requests are sent with the same request_id, but more than 15 minutes apart, idempotency is not guaranteed.

inputFields.name: reservation
inputFields.description: Optional. The reservation that jobs.query request would use. User can specify a reservation to execute the job.query. The expected format is `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/reservations/{reservation}`.

inputFields.name: timeoutMs
inputFields.description: Optional. Optional: Specifies the maximum amount of time, in milliseconds, that the client is willing to wait for the query to complete. By default, this limit is 10 seconds (10,000 milliseconds). If the query is complete, the jobComplete field in the response is true. If the query has not yet completed, jobComplete is false. You can request a longer timeout period in the timeoutMs field. However, the call is not guaranteed to wait for the specified timeout; it typically returns after around 200 seconds (200,000 milliseconds), even if the query is not complete. If jobComplete is false, you can continue to wait for the query to complete by calling the getQueryResults method until the jobComplete field in the getQueryResults response is true.

inputFields.name: useLegacySql
inputFields.description: Specifies whether to use BigQuery's legacy SQL dialect for this query. The default value is true. If set to false, the query uses BigQuery's [GoogleSQL](https://docs.cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/introduction-sql). When useLegacySql is set to false, the value of flattenResults is ignored; query will be run as if flattenResults is false.

inputFields.name: useQueryCache
inputFields.description: Optional. Whether to look for the result in the query cache. The query cache is a best-effort cache that will be flushed whenever tables in the query are modified. The default value is true.

inputFields.name: writeIncrementalResults
inputFields.description: Optional. This is only supported for SELECT query. If set, the query is allowed to write results incrementally to the temporary result table. This may incur a performance penalty. This option cannot be used with Legacy SQL. This feature is not yet available.

types.name: DataFormatOptions_Input
types.description: Options for data format adjustments.

inputFields.name: useInt64Timestamp
inputFields.description: Optional. Output timestamp as usec int64. Default is false.

types.name: mutationInput_bigquery_jobs_query_input_formatOptions_timestampOutputFormat
types.description: Optional. The API output format for a timestamp. This offers more explicit control over the timestamp output format as compared to the existing `use_int64_timestamp` option.

types.name: mutationInput_bigquery_jobs_query_input_jobCreationMode
types.description: Optional. If not set, jobs are always required. If set, the query request will follow the behavior described JobCreationMode.

inputFields.name: bestTrialId
inputFields.description: The best trial_id across all training runs.

inputFields.name: creationTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The time when this model was created, in millisecs since the epoch.

inputFields.name: defaultTrialId
inputFields.description: Output only. The default trial_id to use in TVFs when the trial_id is not passed in. For single-objective [hyperparameter tuning](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery-ml/docs/reference/standard-sql/bigqueryml-syntax-hp-tuning-overview) models, this is the best trial ID. For multi-objective [hyperparameter tuning](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery-ml/docs/reference/standard-sql/bigqueryml-syntax-hp-tuning-overview) models, this is the smallest trial ID among all Pareto optimal trials.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: Optional. A user-friendly description of this model.

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: Output only. A hash of this resource.

inputFields.name: expirationTime
inputFields.description: Optional. The time when this model expires, in milliseconds since the epoch. If not present, the model will persist indefinitely. Expired models will be deleted and their storage reclaimed. The defaultTableExpirationMs property of the encapsulating dataset can be used to set a default expirationTime on newly created models.

inputFields.name: featureColumns
inputFields.description: Output only. Input feature columns for the model inference. If the model is trained with TRANSFORM clause, these are the input of the TRANSFORM clause.

inputFields.name: friendlyName
inputFields.description: Optional. A descriptive name for this model.

inputFields.name: hparamTrials
inputFields.description: Output only. Trials of a [hyperparameter tuning](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery-ml/docs/reference/standard-sql/bigqueryml-syntax-hp-tuning-overview) model sorted by trial_id.

inputFields.name: labelColumns
inputFields.description: Output only. Label columns that were used to train this model. The output of the model will have a "predicted_" prefix to these columns.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: The labels associated with this model. You can use these to organize and group your models. Label keys and values can be no longer than 63 characters, can only contain lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores and dashes. International characters are allowed. Label values are optional. Label keys must start with a letter and each label in the list must have a different key.

inputFields.name: lastModifiedTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The time when this model was last modified, in millisecs since the epoch.

inputFields.name: location
inputFields.description: Output only. The geographic location where the model resides. This value is inherited from the dataset.

inputFields.name: optimalTrialIds
inputFields.description: Output only. For single-objective [hyperparameter tuning](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery-ml/docs/reference/standard-sql/bigqueryml-syntax-hp-tuning-overview) models, it only contains the best trial. For multi-objective [hyperparameter tuning](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery-ml/docs/reference/standard-sql/bigqueryml-syntax-hp-tuning-overview) models, it contains all Pareto optimal trials sorted by trial_id.

inputFields.name: trainingRuns
inputFields.description: Information for all training runs in increasing order of start_time.

inputFields.name: transformColumns
inputFields.description: Output only. This field will be populated if a TRANSFORM clause was used to train a model. TRANSFORM clause (if used) takes feature_columns as input and outputs transform_columns. transform_columns then are used to train the model.

types.name: StandardSqlField_Input

inputFields.name: name

inputFields.name: type
inputFields.description: The data type of a variable such as a function argument. Examples include: * INT64: `{"typeKind": "INT64"}` * ARRAY: { "typeKind": "ARRAY", "arrayElementType": {"typeKind": "STRING"} } * STRUCT>: { "typeKind": "STRUCT", "structType": { "fields": [ { "name": "x", "type": {"typeKind": "STRING"} }, { "name": "y", "type": { "typeKind": "ARRAY", "arrayElementType": {"typeKind": "DATE"} } } ] } } * RANGE: { "typeKind": "RANGE", "rangeElementType": {"typeKind": "DATE"} }

types.name: StandardSqlDataType_Input2
types.description: The data type of a variable such as a function argument. Examples include: * INT64: `{"typeKind": "INT64"}` * ARRAY: { "typeKind": "ARRAY", "arrayElementType": {"typeKind": "STRING"} } * STRUCT>: { "typeKind": "STRUCT", "structType": { "fields": [ { "name": "x", "type": {"typeKind": "STRING"} }, { "name": "y", "type": { "typeKind": "ARRAY", "arrayElementType": {"typeKind": "DATE"} } } ] } } * RANGE: { "typeKind": "RANGE", "rangeElementType": {"typeKind": "DATE"} }

inputFields.name: arrayElementType
inputFields.description: The data type of a variable such as a function argument. Examples include: * INT64: `{"typeKind": "INT64"}` * ARRAY: { "typeKind": "ARRAY", "arrayElementType": {"typeKind": "STRING"} } * STRUCT>: { "typeKind": "STRUCT", "structType": { "fields": [ { "name": "x", "type": {"typeKind": "STRING"} }, { "name": "y", "type": { "typeKind": "ARRAY", "arrayElementType": {"typeKind": "DATE"} } } ] } } * RANGE: { "typeKind": "RANGE", "rangeElementType": {"typeKind": "DATE"} }

inputFields.name: rangeElementType
inputFields.description: The data type of a variable such as a function argument. Examples include: * INT64: `{"typeKind": "INT64"}` * ARRAY: { "typeKind": "ARRAY", "arrayElementType": {"typeKind": "STRING"} } * STRUCT>: { "typeKind": "STRUCT", "structType": { "fields": [ { "name": "x", "type": {"typeKind": "STRING"} }, { "name": "y", "type": { "typeKind": "ARRAY", "arrayElementType": {"typeKind": "DATE"} } } ] } } * RANGE: { "typeKind": "RANGE", "rangeElementType": {"typeKind": "DATE"} }

types.name: RemoteModelInfo_Input
types.description: Remote Model Info

inputFields.name: connection
inputFields.description: Output only. Fully qualified name of the user-provided connection object of the remote model. Format: ```"projects/{project_id}/locations/{location_id}/connections/{connection_id}"```

inputFields.name: endpoint
inputFields.description: Output only. The endpoint for remote model.

inputFields.name: maxBatchingRows

inputFields.name: remoteModelVersion

inputFields.name: speechRecognizer
inputFields.description: Output only. The name of the speech recognizer to use for speech recognition. The expected format is `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/recognizers/{recognizer}`. Customers can specify this field at model creation. If not specified, a default recognizer `projects/{model project}/locations/global/recognizers/_` will be used. See more details at [recognizers](https://cloud.google.com/speech-to-text/v2/docs/reference/rest/v2/projects.locations.recognizers)

types.name: TrainingRun_Input
types.description: Information about a single training query run for the model.

inputFields.name: classLevelGlobalExplanations
inputFields.description: Output only. Global explanation contains the explanation of top features on the class level. Applies to classification models only.

inputFields.name: results
inputFields.description: Output only. Output of each iteration run, results.size() <= max_iterations.

inputFields.name: startTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The start time of this training run.

inputFields.name: trainingStartTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The start time of this training run, in milliseconds since epoch.

inputFields.name: vertexAiModelId
inputFields.description: The model id in the [Vertex AI Model Registry](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/model-registry/introduction) for this training run.

inputFields.name: vertexAiModelVersion
inputFields.description: Output only. The model version in the [Vertex AI Model Registry](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/model-registry/introduction) for this training run.

types.name: GlobalExplanation_Input
types.description: Global explanations containing the top most important features after training.

inputFields.name: classLabel
inputFields.description: Class label for this set of global explanations. Will be empty/null for binary logistic and linear regression models. Sorted alphabetically in descending order.

inputFields.name: explanations
inputFields.description: A list of the top global explanations. Sorted by absolute value of attribution in descending order.

types.name: Explanation_Input

inputFields.name: attribution
inputFields.description: Attribution of feature.

inputFields.name: featureName
inputFields.description: The full feature name. For non-numerical features, will be formatted like `.`. Overall size of feature name will always be truncated to first 120 characters.

inputFields.name: transformSql

types.name: Routine_Input
types.description: A user-defined function or a stored procedure.

inputFields.name: arguments
inputFields.description: Optional.

inputFields.name: creationTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The time when this routine was created, in milliseconds since the epoch.

inputFields.name: definitionBody
inputFields.description:
Required. The body of the routine. For functions, this is the expression in the AS clause. If `language = "SQL"`, it is the substring inside (but excluding) the parentheses. For example, for the function created with the following statement: `CREATE FUNCTION JoinLines(x string, y string) as (concat(x, "
", y))` The definition_body is `concat(x, "
", y)` (
 is not replaced with linebreak). If `language="JAVASCRIPT"`, it is the evaluated string in the AS clause. For example, for the function created with the following statement: `CREATE FUNCTION f() RETURNS STRING LANGUAGE js AS 'return "
";
'` The definition_body is `return "
";
` Note that both 
 are replaced with linebreaks. If `definition_body` references another routine, then that routine must be fully qualified with its project ID.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: Optional. The description of the routine, if defined.

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: Output only. A hash of this resource.

inputFields.name: importedLibraries
inputFields.description: Optional. If language = "JAVASCRIPT", this field stores the path of the imported JAVASCRIPT libraries.

inputFields.name: lastModifiedTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The time when this routine was last modified, in milliseconds since the epoch.

inputFields.name: strictMode
inputFields.description: Optional. Use this option to catch many common errors. Error checking is not exhaustive, and successfully creating a procedure doesn't guarantee that the procedure will successfully execute at runtime. If `strictMode` is set to `TRUE`, the procedure body is further checked for errors such as non-existent tables or columns. The `CREATE PROCEDURE` statement fails if the body fails any of these checks. If `strictMode` is set to `FALSE`, the procedure body is checked only for syntax. For procedures that invoke themselves recursively, specify `strictMode=FALSE` to avoid non-existent procedure errors during validation. Default value is `TRUE`.

types.name: Argument_Input
types.description: Input/output argument of a function or a stored procedure.

inputFields.name: isAggregate
inputFields.description: Optional. Whether the argument is an aggregate function parameter. Must be Unset for routine types other than AGGREGATE_FUNCTION. For AGGREGATE_FUNCTION, if set to false, it is equivalent to adding "NOT AGGREGATE" clause in DDL; Otherwise, it is equivalent to omitting "NOT AGGREGATE" clause in DDL.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Optional. The name of this argument. Can be absent for function return argument.

types.name: RoutineBuildStatus_Input
types.description: The status of a routine build.

inputFields.name: buildDuration
inputFields.description: Output only. The time taken for the image build. Populated only after the build succeeds or fails.

inputFields.name: buildStateUpdateTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The time when the build state was updated last.

inputFields.name: imageSizeBytes
inputFields.description: Output only. The size of the image in bytes. Populated only after the build succeeds.

types.name: ExternalRuntimeOptions_Input

inputFields.name: containerCpu
inputFields.description: Optional. Amount of CPU provisioned for a Python UDF container instance. For more information, see [Configure container limits for Python UDFs](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/user-defined-functions-python#configure-container-limits)

inputFields.name: containerMemory
inputFields.description: Optional. Amount of memory provisioned for a Python UDF container instance. Format: {number}{unit} where unit is one of "M", "G", "Mi" and "Gi" (e.g. 1G, 512Mi). If not specified, the default value is 512Mi. For more information, see [Configure container limits for Python UDFs](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/user-defined-functions-python#configure-container-limits)

inputFields.name: maxBatchingRows
inputFields.description: Optional. Maximum number of rows in each batch sent to the external runtime. If absent or if 0, BigQuery dynamically decides the number of rows in a batch.

inputFields.name: runtimeConnection
inputFields.description: Optional. Fully qualified name of the connection whose service account will be used to execute the code in the container. Format: ```"projects/{project_id}/locations/{location_id}/connections/{connection_id}"```

inputFields.name: runtimeVersion
inputFields.description: Optional. Language runtime version. Example: `python-3.11`.

types.name: PythonOptions_Input

inputFields.description: Required. The name of the function defined in Python code as the entry point when the Python UDF is invoked.

inputFields.name: packages

For more information, see [Use third-party packages](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/user-defined-functions-python#third-party-packages).

inputFields.description: User-defined context as a set of key/value pairs, which will be sent as function invocation context together with batched arguments in the requests to the remote service. The total number of bytes of keys and values must be less than 8KB.
